title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €75k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: Analyst (Capital Markets & Portfolio Management)
description: **Job title** Analyst – Capital Markets \& Portfolio Management **About us** I\-RES is the leading provider of quality private residential rental accommodation in Ireland with almost 3,600 homes in communities across Dublin. We are committed to excellent service, operating responsibly, minimising our environmental impact, and maximising our contribution to the community. Our shares are listed on the Main Securities Market of Euronext Dublin. More information at www.iresreit.ie **Contract Type** Full\-time, permanent, 9am to 5:30pm, Monday to Friday. **Flexibility** This is a predominately office\-based role and following successful completion of a probationary period, the typical working week is 4 days at the office, with the option of 1 day working from home. The role will also involve some travel to assets in the I\-RES portfolio within Dublin. **Location** South Dock House, Grand Canal, Dublin 2\. **Job Description** The Capital Markets \& Portfolio Management team are seeking to appoint an Analyst to support the execution of the Company’s investment, capital deployment and portfolio management strategy. The team is a small, highly collaborative and commercially focused business unit with responsibility for sourcing, underwriting and executing transactions, as well as supporting the active management of the existing portfolio. The team also works closely with senior management and across the wider business on investment strategy, valuations, capital allocation, corporate initiatives, investor engagement and ESG. This role is suited to a candidate with at least two years’ relevant professional experience, who is looking to take on increased responsibility, develop transaction and portfolio experience, and progress their career within a listed real estate platform. **Key Responsibilities** The Analyst will have responsibility for the following: * Supporting transaction sourcing, market intelligence and relationship management with agents, advisors and counterparties. * Financial analysis and underwriting of potential investment, development and capital expenditure opportunities, including the preparation of detailed financial models and investment appraisals. * Supporting and, where appropriate, leading elements of transaction execution, working closely with external legal, tax, valuation and technical advisors. * Preparing and contributing to investment committee papers, business cases, internal reports and ad hoc analysis for senior management and the Board. * Supporting portfolio\-level analysis, monitoring and forecasting investment performance, monitoring and forecasting environmental performance, strategic asset reviews and business planning. · Contributing to asset management and capital projects, including refurbishments, leasing initiatives, sustainability and ESG\-related capital expenditure. · Assisting with governance and reporting for budgeted capital expenditure, including assisting the team in preparing, planning and presenting projects for approval. * Assisting with valuations, market analysis and corporate initiatives, including capital markets activity and investor\-related workstreams. * Supporting other team members and contributing positively to a collaborative, high\-performance team culture. **Education, Skills, Experience \& Personal Characteristics** We will consider candidates from a range of relevant backgrounds. The below reflects the typical skills and experience expected for this role rather than an exhaustive list. **Education \& Qualifications** * Third level degree (and / or masters) in a relevant discipline such as Real Estate, Finance, Business, Economics, Engineering or Maths. * Professionally qualified, or working towards, SCSI / RICS / CFA / ACA is preferred. **Skills \& Experience** * Minimum of 2 years’ relevant professional experience in real estate, capital markets, investment, valuation, banking, advisory or a related field. * Strong financial and analytical capability with a high level of numerical competence. * Experience building and operating advanced excel\-based financial models is essential. * A good understanding of real estate investment fundamentals, including pricing, yields, cash flows and valuation concepts. * An understanding and interest in the listed real estate investment sector. * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to develop market leading presentations, business plans, board level investment papers and present the analysis clearly and concisely. * Ability to work independently, on a self\-directed basis and consistently deliver high quality output and productivity. * Experience working with professional advisors and contributing to live transactions or asset management projects. * Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint. * Experience using AI tools to support professional tasks a benefit. * Full driver’s licence preferred. **Personal Characteristics** * A strong interest in real estate, capital markets and portfolio strategy. * Commercially minded, proactive and able to work with a high degree of autonomy. * Detail\-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy and quality of output. * Able to manage multiple workstreams and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment. * A collaborative team player with the confidence to engage constructively with senior stakeholders. **Benefits** * Competitive salary, commensurate with experience and an annual bonus structure based on individualised performance objectives * 5% company pension contribution which can be matched by at least a 3% employee contribution * Company paid private health insurance * 23 days paid annual leave * Strong focus on personal development and career progression, including support for professional qualifications (e.g. SCSI / RICS, CFA) * Diverse and inclusive team – Silver Investors in Diversity award * Active social and volunteering programme * Bike to Work and Public Transport TaxSaver schemes * Vouched business expenses

title: Application Support Consultant - Hybrid

Louth (Hybrid – 3 Days per Week Onsite) **About the Role** An established and growing software company is seeking an Application Support Consultant to join its Customer Success function. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys solving technical problems, working closely with customers, and developing expertise across a broad range of business systems and technologies. title: Assistant Vice President – Corporate Secretarial Services (COE)
description: **About Waystone** Waystone is a leading asset\-servicing solutions provider of institutional governance, administration, risk and compliance services to financial institutions. With over 25 years’ experience and a comprehensive range of specialist services to its name, Waystone helps our clients structure, operate and grow through our expertise, innovation and digitisation, backed by the operational scale to support global expansion **Summary:**Reporting to the Associate Director – Corporate Secretarial Services (COE), the Assistant Vice President – Corporate Secretarial Services (COE) will be an integral part of the team’s success. **ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES** The successful candidate will be responsible for a portfolio of fund clients, primarily domiciled in the Cayman Islands, Bermuda, the U.S. and/or BVI, will manage multiple client relationships, will review work produced by others within the team and will assist with training others within the team. Responsibilities may include (but are not limited to) the following: * Build a relationship with the stakeholders of funds. * Organisation of Board, Board Committee and shareholder meetings, including the production/ review of materials in advance of such meetings and minutes thereafter. * Assist with changes to funds’ Boards of directors, service providers etc. and the necessary notifications and documents relating thereto. * Ensure that all actions taken, and agreements entered into by funds, are duly considered and authorised by the Board of directors. * Assist with the approval of financial statements and assist clients with conflicts of interest registers and Board assessments. * Provide reporting to the management of the team. * Update client records. * Assist with project work, as directed by the management of the team. * Produce and/ or review draft resolutions, as required. * Liaise with registered offices in respect of requisite filings to be made for each fund and to make and/or monitor all necessary registrations with governmental and similar agencies in the jurisdiction of each fund. * Assist with establishing relationships with service providers on behalf of funds. * Assist with the liquidation process of funds which have ceased trading. * Assist with the training of other members of the team. **REQUIREMENTS** * A Bachelor’s degree. * An excellent command of the English language. * At least four years of relevant, funds related, work experience. * The candidate should be self\-motivated and will show an ability to work in a demanding and progressive environment, meeting deadlines as necessary. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.

title: Associate – Financial Regulation (3-5PQE)
description: ### **Description** **Background** Our market\-leading Financial Regulation practice advises a wide range of domestic and international financial institutions, including credit institutions, investment firms, UCITS management companies, AIFMs, payment institutions, e\-money institutions, non\-bank lenders and insurance companies. Due to continued growth in our Financial Regulation group, we are seeking a talented and ambitious Associate (3–5 PQE) to join our team. **About the Role** This is an excellent opportunity to work within a top\-tier Financial Regulation team, advising on high\-profile and complex regulatory matters in a dynamic and evolving legal landscape. You will work closely with partners and senior associates on advisory and transactional regulatory matters as well as regulatory investigations. You will have direct contact with clients and strong exposure to ongoing business development initiatives. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Advise financial institutions on a wide range of Irish and EU regulatory frameworks * Support on licensing, authorisation, and ongoing supervisory matters with the Central Bank of Ireland (including investigations and enforcement cases) * Provide regulatory input into corporate transactions, including M\&A and restructuring projects * Assist clients with compliance frameworks, governance structures, and regulatory change programmes * Contribute to thought leadership, client briefings, and internal knowledge development * Advise on a wide range of financial services regulation including MiFID II, CRD/CRR (including CRD VI), PSD2/PSD3, and other EU directives/regulations * Support clients through regulatory authorisations, approvals, and ongoing supervisory engagement * Draft and review policies, procedures, and regulatory documentation * Provide strategic regulatory advice for new products, services, and market entry * Work collaboratively with colleagues across practice areas including Corporate/M\&A, Debt Finance and Funds * Managing client relationships and contributing to business development initiatives **What Success Looks Like*** Strong technical competence in financial regulation and ability to deliver clear, pragmatic advice * Building trusted relationships with clients and becoming a key contact on matters * Effective management of matters, delivering high\-quality work within deadlines * Active contribution to team knowledge, training, and profile\-raising initiatives * Demonstrating commercial awareness and understanding of clients’ business needs **Why Join Us?*** Opportunity to work with a leading Financial Regulation team * Exposure to high\-quality, complex and international work * Strong focus on career development, training, and progression * Collaborative and inclusive team environment * Access to cutting\-edge regulatory work at the forefront of market developments ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Essential*** Qualified solicitor with 3–5 years’ PQE in financial regulation * Experience gained in a top\-tier law firm or in\-house legal/regulatory environment * Strong knowledge of EU financial services regulatory frameworks * Excellent drafting, analytical, and problem\-solving skills * Strong communication skills and ability to build client relationships * Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple priorities **Desirable*** Experience advising on Irish financial services regulatory frameworks and processes * Exposure to cross\-border regulatory matters * Interest in emerging areas such as digital finance, and fintech ### **Benefits** * Competitive salary and bonus structure * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits * Professional development opportunities * Hybrid working arrangements ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Azure Cloud Engineer - 12 month contract

description: At Insomnia we are serious about Coffee! Established in Galway in 1997, the first independent coffee shop company in Ireland. Crafted the Irish way, our coffee is bold, unapologetic, yet super smooth with just the right amount of character. We're growing our business and always looking for enthusiastic coffee lovers who can deliver on the Insomnia philosophy; that you'll get the best cup of coffee every time you visit our stores. In return, we can offer you a great work environment, where you can master your coffee and customer service skills, along with opportunities to develop and progress your career with us as we continue to grow. Why Join us? At Insomnia Coffee, we believe great coffee starts with great people! Here's what you can look forward to when you join our team: Competitive hourly rates Flexible work patterns to suit your lifestyle Free meal on every shift Opportunities for career growth and development Access to a fantastic Employee Assistance Programme 31 minimum contracted hours Insomnia Baristas Bring a positive attitude and take pride in delivering great service with every cup Are team players who enjoy a fast paced, customer focused environment Have experience in high standard, fast paced settings, ideally with knowledge of audits and compliance (but don't worry if you don't\-we'll train you!) Can work flexible hours, including weekends, across our 7 day operation Have coffee experience\-but it's not a dealbreaker. We'll provide full training to help you shine! If you're energetic, enthusiastic, and love the idea of making someone's day with great coffee and a warm smile, we'd love to hear from you. Location: Unit 2 Lidl Neighbourhood Centre, Strand Rd, Portmarnock, Co. Dublin

title: Better Energy Homes (BEH) - Programme Executive
description: ### **Job Title** Better Energy (BEH) \- Programme Executive ### **Salary** €53,071pa (Other arrangements may apply if you are currently a serving civil or public servant) ### **Tenure** 5\-year fixed tern contract ### **Work Location** Dublin, Dundalk, Cork of Sligo office ### **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 18th June 2026 ### **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in the Better Energy Homes (BEH) Team in the Delivery Department of the National Retrofit Directorate. The Better Energy Homes Programme is focussed on supporting Ireland in reaching its Climate Action Plan goals for 2030 and beyond by offering a range of individual energy efficiency upgrade grants to residential property owners. The position will play a key role in supporting the Better Energy Homes Engineer and Programme Manager with the day\-to\-day management of the scheme and supporting the role out of new initiatives and systems, underpinned by Governance and Compliance structures. As Programme Executive for the Better Energy Homes Programme, you will be involved in the day to day running of the Programme including ongoing quality management, ensuring efficient Programme processes, development of continuous process improvements, development of IT reports, liaising with third party stakeholders, among other duties. This position will report to the Better Energy Homes Engineer. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI's targets under the Climate Action Plan. ### **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * An honours degree (a minimum NFQ level 8 or greater) in a relevant discipline such as Engineering, Architecture, Quantity Surveying, Energy Management, and minimum of 3 years directly relevant experience working in a similar programme or on retrofitting or energy\-related projects. OR * An ordinary degree (a minimum NFQ level 7 or greater) in a relevant discipline such as Engineering, Architecture, Quantity Surveying, Energy Management, and minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in a similar programme or on retrofitting or energy\-related projects. OR * A third level certificate (a minimum NFQ Level 6 or greater) in a relevant discipline such as Engineering, Architecture, Quantity Surveying, Energy Management, and minimum of 8 years directly relevant experience working in a similar programme or on retrofitting or energy\-related projects. * Experience of working in cross functional teams developing organisational relationships. * Strong project management skills and knowledge of technical development, including change management, scope, risk, and process management. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including report writing, presenting, public speaking, and the ability to communicate complex concepts to both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Well\-developed IT skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, SharePoint). ### **Desirable Requirements** * A post\-graduate qualification in a relevant discipline such as Engineering, Architecture, Quantity Surveying, Energy Management. * Experience in supporting IT projects. * Experience in developing Power BI reports and managing dashboard reporting. * Experience of lean process management. * Experience of working in energy\-related policy environment across the public sector, academia or industry. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. ### **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate's application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available here. **\#LI\-Hybrid**

title: Bid Coordinator
description: **Overview** At Sisk, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. We are seeking a highly organised and detail‑focused Bid Coordinator to support our Bid Manager in delivering high‑quality, compliant tender submissions for civil engineering and infrastructure projects. In this role, you will play a key part in administering documentation, managing templates, tracking deliverables, and coordinating inputs from multiple stakeholders. You will help ensure that all tender materials are accurate, well‑presented, and submitted on time. **Responsibilities** * Support the preparation of PQQs and tender submissions for civil engineering and infrastructure projects. * Help manage bid timelines by tracking deliverables, clarifications, and document workflows. * Format, compile, and maintain tender documents, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and correct version control. * Gather inputs from estimators, planners, designers, subcontractors, and other contributors. * Maintain and update CVs, organisational charts, project references, and other standard bid materials. * Upload and submit tender documents through client portals and ensure compliance with public procurement requirements, including ESPD/ESPD‑Ireland. * Assist in developing and maintaining bid libraries, templates, trackers, and evidence folders. * Support the creation of clear and concise written content and improve visuals/layouts where needed. * Coordinate tender queries, collect responses, and keep stakeholders informed throughout the process. * Handle post‑submission tasks such as portal uploads, data entry, and document archiving. **Experience** * Proven experience in a bid support, document control, project administrative or coordination role. * Demonstrable experience working on civil engineering tenders or technical submissions. * Experience managing CV templates, organisational charts, subcontractor information, and compliance schedules (ref. RFT 7088144 personnel requirements \& CV templates). **Qualifications** **Required** * Relevant third level qualification in Engineering, Construction Management, Business, Communications, or similar. * Training in document control, MS Office, SharePoint/Teams or bid management tools an advantage. * Strong organisational skills, with ability to track tender deliverables, clarifications and workflows. * Excellent document formatting and version control skills. * Ability to coordinate inputs from multiple stakeholders (estimators, planners, designers, subcontractors). * Knowledge of Irish public procurement requirements and ESPD/ESPD‑Ireland formats. **Desirable** * Experience compiling tender programmes, trackers and evidence folders (seen repeatedly in tender email chains). * Experience with graphics/visual enhancements for submission documents. **Additional Information** **What We Offer** At Sisk, your rewards package goes far beyond your salary. Our most valued benefits include: * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave * Pension scheme * Comprehensive health, wellbeing and lifestyle supports * Career development opportunities * And much more as part of our Total Rewards programme **Our People \& Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We offer the opportunity to work with purpose, grow a career without limits and be part of a supportive, collaborative team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited CVs or speculative contact from unapproved agencies are not accepted, and no fees will be payable. **Equity, Inclusion \& Accessibility** We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion, family status or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process to support all candidates. **Encouraging All Talent to Apply** We understand that the confidence gap or imposter syndrome can discourage great people from applying. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to express your interest — your skills, experience and potential are what matter. **Compliance \& Transparency** All recruitment materials are designed to meet or exceed legal and regulatory requirements, including the Irish and UK Equality Acts and the EU Pay Transparency Directive, ensuring fairness, transparency and equal access for every applicant.

title: Binance Accelerator Program - Compliance Training
description: Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300\+ million people in 100\+ countries for our industry\-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital\-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world. **About Binance Accelerator Program** Binance Accelerator Program is a concise fixed\-term program designed for Early Career Talent to have an immersive experience in the rapidly expanding Web3 space. You will be given the opportunity to experience life at Binance and understand what goes on behind the scenes of the worlds’ leading blockchain ecosystem. Alongside your job, there will also be a focus on networking and development, which will expand your professional network and build transferable skills to propel you forward in your career. Learn about BAP Program HERE **Who May Apply** Current university students and recent graduates We are seeking a motivated, detail\-oriented, and AI\-ready Training Intern to support our Compliance training and employee development initiatives. This role offers a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in instructional design, training operations, and learning analytics within the fast\-growing cryptocurrency and digital assets industry. As part of the team, you will contribute to building training programs that are engaging, effective, and aligned with evolving regulatory expectations while leveraging cutting\-edge AI tools to enhance learning outcomes. Ideal candidates are self\-motivated, well\-organized, eager to deepen their knowledge of training within a compliance\-focused cryptocurrency environment, and ready to leverage AI and modern technology to drive innovation in learning and development. **Responsibilities:** **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Partner with Product, Compliance, and Customer Support teams to ensure training content is accurate, relevant, and aligned with business and regulatory priorities * Support global stakeholders across different regions and time zones **Learning Content Development (AI\-Enhanced)** * Design and develop engaging learning materials (e\-learning modules, guides, tutorials, job aids) * Leverage AI tools (e.g., generative AI, content automation, voice/video tools) to enhance content creation, personalization, and scalability * Support the development of interactive and scenario\-based learning experiences **Training Delivery \& Facilitation** * Coordinate and support virtual and in\-person training sessions, workshops, and labs * Enhance learner engagement through modern delivery techniques and digital tools **Training Compliance \& Monitoring** * Track employee completion of mandatory training programs using LMS tools * Follow up with employees and stakeholders to ensure timely completion and compliance adherence **Training Effectiveness \& Data Analytics** * Measure training effectiveness through data analysis, feedback, and assessment results * Generate insights to improve learning outcomes and support continuous improvement initiatives **Learner Support \& Experience** * Provide personalized support to employees facing challenges in completing training * Recommend alternative learning approaches or resources to improve completion rates and engagement **AI \& Innovation in Learning** * Explore and implement AI\-driven learning solutions within the team (e.g., adaptive learning, automated content generation, AI chatbots) * Continuously identify opportunities to improve efficiency, scalability, and learner experience through technology **Ad\-hoc Support** * Assist the QA \& Training Manager with ongoing projects and strategic initiatives **Essential Skills \& Experience:** * Fluency in English (written and verbal), proficiency in a second language is a plus. * Strong attention to detail, data\-driven mindset, and excellent communication skills * Ability to work across functional and geographic boundaries in flexible hours * Proficient in using various digital tools for content creation (PowerPoint, Active Presenter, video editing software) * Demonstrates learning agility, critical thinking, and an innovative mindset * Results\-driven attitude with confidence and a penchant for finding creative solutions **Preferred Qualifications:** * Interest in compliance, law, or regulation in crypto/fintech * Previous experience in compliance operations, training, or learning \& development teams * Familiarity with AI tools (e.g., generative AI platforms, Claw, automation tools, ChatGPT) * Experience with Learning Management Systems (LMS) or training platforms Why Binance * Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem * Collaborate with world\-class talent in a user\-centric global organization with a flat structure * Tackle unique, fast\-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment * Thrive in a results\-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning * Competitive salary and company benefits * Work\-from\-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team) Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success. *By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our Candidate Privacy Notice .* We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Bridge Design Engineer - Dublin or Cork
description: Roughan \& O’Donovan (ROD) is looking to recruit a talented bridge design engineer with three to seven years’ relevant experience for a permanent position within our bridge and civil\-structural engineering team **Job type:** Permanent **Location:** Dublin or Cork **Salary:** Competitive **About the role** ------------------ As an experienced bridge design engineer, you will provide input to our bridge and multidisciplinary civil\-structural engineering projects. You will be supported throughout by senior members of our bridge teams in Ireland and the UK. Current and recent projects include: * DART\+ West, Ireland * Waterford City Public Infrastructure Project, Ireland * Narrow Water Bridge Project, Ireland * George’s Dock Bridge, Ireland * Herring Bridge, Great Yarmouth, UK * Renfrew Bridge, Renfrewshire, Scotland * Whitegates to Athlone Castle Cycleway, Athlone, Ireland * A6 Dungiven to Drumahoe dualling scheme, Northern Ireland **Main duties and responsibilities** ------------------------------------ * Work with existing and new bridge and civil structures across a range of materials and forms, superstructure, substructure, foundation and articulation elements * Participate in early project stages, including scoping, specifying and interpreting surveys, inspection and investigative works, optioneering and scheme design * Undertake preliminary design and detailed design work, including structural analytical modelling, calculations etc. * Assist in the production of drawings * Prepare documents, including technical reports, specifications and contract documents * Support the team to deliver projects within agreed timeframes * Undertake independent design checking and provide construction stage technical support * Interface with clients from local and national authorities, consultants and design\-and\-build contractors amongst others **Essential requirements** -------------------------- * Honours degree in civil/structural engineering (Level 8 in the National Framework of Qualifications or equivalent) * A minimum of three years' post\-qualification experience in the detailed design of bridges * Ability to undertake bridge structure analytical modelling, including construction stage and finite element analysis * Good knowledge of structural analysis software including finite element analysis software * Good technical knowledge of bridge design * Excellent attention to the detailing of bridge structures * Experience of written and in\-person interaction with external parties * Flexible, motivated and focused * A strong work ethic * Fluent or highly proficient in spoken and written English **Desirable** ------------- * Good knowledge of and experience in the use of the Design Manual for Roads and Bridges (DMRB) and other standards and legislation in the field of bridge/structural design * Working knowledge of Eurocodes in the field of bridge design * Excellent numerical and analytical skills * Experience of working independently on detailed bridge design * Experience of working in a multidisciplinary environment * Experience of working to tight deadlines **About Roughan \& O’Donovan** ------------------------------ Roughan \& O’Donovan (ROD) is expert in planning, designing and managing major infrastructure projects for public and private sector clients across Ireland and the UK. An independently owned firm, we employ a team of more than 250 people, of over 20 different nationalities, in our Dublin, Cork and UK offices. Our hands\-on, director\-led approach to projects and operations enables us to work collaboratively with our team to develop innovative, cost\-effective solutions that maximise value for our clients and learning for our people. We enjoy strong client, partner and stakeholder relationships. Our people are the heart of our business, and we invest heavily in their personal and professional development through all stages of career development. We are an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer and operate an Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE) training scheme. We have comprehensive student placement, internship, graduate and chartership support programmes to help our team reach their full potential, as individuals and as engineers. We offer health and wellbeing packages, flexible working and life\-event supports, so whatever challenges life may bring, you can feel secure in the knowledge that you will be taken care of. We are a friendly team with an active social committee. From surfing weekends and ski events to tag rugby and five\-a\-side tournaments, you'll find lots of ways to get involved. Roughan \& O’Donovan is an equal opportunity employer. Roughan \& O'Donovan will retain, for at least one year, all records arising through the recruitment process for compliance purposes in line with the Employment Equality Acts, 1998\-2015\. Roughan \& O'Donovan is not accepting unsolicited CVs from search firms. CVs submitted by search firms without a valid written search agreement, including those submitted to hiring managers, are deemed to be the sole property of Roughan \& O'Donovan, and no fee will be paid in the event of candidate(s) being hired.

title: Business Development & Campaign Operations Specialist
description: Job Purpose Drive growth through market research, AI\-driven campaign architecture, and operational administration. Working on\-site with the Operations Team, you will design, execute, and monitor multi\-channel campaigns while supporting internal departments to streamline business processes. Key Responsibilities * **Business Development \& Research:** Conduct market research and data analysis to identify target demographics. Formulate research\-backed plans for upcoming marketing campaigns, utilizing advanced AI methodologies to map structures and forecast performance metrics. * **Campaign Rollouts \& Administration:** Execute multi\-channel outreach spanning social media, email, and outbound calls. Collaborate with Creative, Sales, and Tech departments to manage end\-to\-end rollouts. Build and maintain customized Gemini Gem Agents and AI workflows to optimize planning, script generation, and customer segmentation. Track workflows using centralized management tools. * **Business Process Support:** Evaluate and optimize core business processes across all internal departments to troubleshoot bottlenecks and introduce automation. Manage the digital architecture of events using the OpenWater platform. Oversee backend administration, content updates, and operational health of WordPress websites. Supervise Media Monitoring tools to track brand sentiment. * **Creative Design \& Implementation:** End\-to\-end creation and management of social media campaigns. Apply editorial design principles to create visually compelling layouts for magazine advertising and digital publications. Execute high\-level word processing and reporting. * **Training \& Upskilling:** Design and deliver internal training programs to upskill staff across all departments on new AI tools, software platforms, and standardized operating procedures (SOPs). Job Requirements \& Qualifications * **Experience:** 2\+ years of experience in business development support, marketing operations, or campaign administration. Proven track record of supporting cross\-functional teams and optimizing business processes. Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, IT, or a related field preferred. * **Project Management:** Advanced proficiency in Monday.com for setting up operational dashboards, tracking cross\-departmental KPIs, and managing workflows. * **AI Tools:** Hands\-on experience in Gemini Gem Agent creation and prompt engineering for business research and structural planning. * **Event Tech:** Administrative experience with OpenWater or equivalent enterprise ecosystems. * **Web \& Content:** Technical familiarity with WordPress website administration and professional word\-processing software. * **Productivity Suites:** Expert proficiency in G Suite (Google Workspace) for documentation and cross\-team data management. * **Design \& Media:** Practical experience in magazine advertising editorial design, layout generation, and media monitoring supervision. Pay: €36,000\.00\-€36,605\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Business Development Analyst
description: Note: This role is on\-site in Palma, Mallorca **What is XFuel anyway? 🚀🌳⛽** At XFuel, we’re not just imagining a net\-zero future — we’re building it. With patented waste\-to\-fuel technology, multiple pilot plants deployed, strong investor and EU backing, and a mission that matters, we’re scaling rapidly to decarbonise global transport. Join a high\-impact, innovation\-driven team where your work directly contributes to one of the world’s most urgent challenges. We offer a collaborative culture, tailored relocation package, and a highly competitive market salary. If you’re driven, sharp, and hungry to tackle one of the world’s toughest problems, jump in! Let’s build the future **🚀** **What is my mission? 👩 💻👨 💻** Your mission is to support the growth of XFuel's commercial pipeline by conducting market research, identifying new business opportunities, and providing essential deal support to the business development team. You'll help source potential partnerships, conduct stakeholder research, and support deal structuring, enabling the team to close high\-impact partnerships and customer contracts. This is a hands\-on, research\-focused role that's fundamental to scaling our commercial success. You'll work across multiple geographies and customer segments, gaining exposure to the full BD lifecycle while building the skills needed to grow in business development. **Specific responsibilities will include:** * **Research \& Sourcing:** Identify and profile potential customers, partners, and suppliers across maritime sector. Conduct market analysis to support BD strategy. * **Opportunity Management:** Maintain and organize the BD pipeline; track opportunities from initial lead through deal closure (supporting senior team members in structuring and negotiation). * **Initial Customer Outreach:** Conduct first\-contact research and outreach to prospective customers and partners; qualify leads and prepare briefs for senior team follow\-up. * **Deal Support:** Prepare deal materials, data rooms, and documentation; assist with competitive analysis, contract templates, and project tracking. * **Stakeholder Research:** Profile key decision\-makers, corporate structures, and strategic priorities for priority accounts and potential partners. * **Cross\-functional Collaboration:** Work with technical, commercial, and operations teams to gather information needed for customer pitches and partnership proposals. * **Pipeline Visibility:** Maintain accurate records and reporting on BD activity; support forecasting and strategy discussions with leadership. **We’re looking for someone who: 🧠💚🥇** * **Early\-stage talent:** 2\+ years of experience in business development, sales support, market research, or related commercial roles (internships and entry\-level positions count). * **Research skills:** Strong ability to conduct market research, stakeholder analysis, and competitive intelligence; comfortable using LinkedIn, company databases, and public sources. * **Organizational mindset:** Detail\-oriented, with ability to manage multiple opportunities and maintain accurate pipeline records. * **Communication skills:** Clear written and verbal communication; able to draft professional emails, briefs, and research summaries. * **AI\-native working style: Comfortable using AI tools (such as Claude) to accelerate research, draft communications, and support analysis. Able to apply AI to practical BD tasks, synthesising market intelligence, preparing partner briefings, and supporting reporting workflows rather than relying solely on manual methods.** * **Self\-starter:** Proactive in identifying opportunities and solving problems; thrives with minimal supervision. * **Resilient and adaptable:** Comfortable in a fast\-paced startup environment with changing priorities; views obstacles as learning opportunities. * **Team player:** Collaborates effectively across functions; eager to support colleagues and learn from experienced team members. * **Sustainability\-focused:** Genuinely passionate about climate action and XFuel's mission to decarbonize transportation. * **EU eligibility:** Eligible to work and live in the EU. **It would be beneficial to have familiarity with: 🧠🥈** * Previous experience in early\-stage startups or scale\-ups. * Familiarity with maritime, energy, or industrial sectors. * Experience with CRM tools or BD pipeline management platforms. * Any background in climate tech or sustainability. **Benefits 💰** * High\-impact mission and purpose\-driven team / culture * Competitive salary * *Temporary housing assistance while you search for a place of your own* * Startup equity \+ incentives * Enhanced parental leave * Monthly Team lunches and socials * Working from one of the most incredible places to live! **Location 🌍** On\-site in Palma de Mallorca, Spain (GMT \+1\) – It doesn’t get any better! **Our Values 🙌** We innovate to better serve our planet – we believe innovation is crucial in tackling climate change. We focus on impact – we work on problems that have the biggest impact. We act with integrity – do what’s right, even when it’s hard. We are passionate and win together – we know that success is a result of great teams, positive energy and collaboration **What if you’re a partial fit? 🌱** We prioritise grit, positivity, and the willingness to get stuck in, and encourage you to apply even if your experience doesn't exactly match this job description. **Equal employment opportunity 🌈** XFuel is an equal opportunity employer: we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

title: Business Development Representative (UK/IE Markets)
description: **Market Development Representative (UK/IE Market) \- Dublin, Ireland** Sales \& Business Development Dublin, Ireland \- Hybrid **Company Overview** Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). **What you’ll do** As a Market Development Development Representative (MDR), you will be responsible for generating qualified opportunities that progress through the sales funnel and ultimately generate new revenue to grow our presence in EMEA. This position is an individual contributor reporting to the Market development Manager and is designated **Hybrid** . **Responsibility** * You will identify and generate new business opportunities and pipeline within a defined territory. * Strategically manage and plan your territory and outbound strategy. * Spend the majority of your time managing multi\-channel outbound prospecting to educate potential customers on what Docusign has to offer and gain insight into their business needs. * You will drive brand and product awareness in the **UK/IE market** through regular email and call campaigns. * Schedule high\-impact appointments with those who are of good potential with Account Executives. * Record prospect information in our CRM database. * Present a balanced approach to cold calling, relationship development, sales process, and appointment setting. **Job Designation** **Hybrid:** Employees divide their time between in\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: **Minimum 3 days per week** in\-office expectation) Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. **What you bring** **Basic** * **Fluency in English is required.** * Understanding of sales cycles and solution\-selling tactics * Proficiency in Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other core business applications * Ability to quickly learn and adopt new technologies in a fast\-paced, AI\-first environment **Preferred** * Track record of success in outbound sales/business development role * Confident and courteous verbal and written communication skills * Excellent organizational skills * Familiarity with AI\-powered sales, research, and productivity tools, including Gong, LinkedIn, Glean, Gemini, Notebook LM * BS/BA degree or equivalent professional experience **Life at DocuSign** **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal. We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live.

title: CIO Regulated Payments
description: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) is the world's leading provider of online travel and related services, provided to consumers and local partners in more than 220 countries and territories through five primary consumer\-facing brands: Booking.com, Priceline, Agoda, KAYAK and OpenTable. The mission of Booking Holdings is to make it easier for everyone to experience the world. For more information, visit BookingHoldings.com and follow us on X (formerly known as Twitter) @BookingHoldings. Booking Holdings Financial Services (hereinafter “BHFS”) provides financial services in the form of payment services and e\-money products across all the brands. BHFS is committed to conducting its business in compliance with applicable laws, regulations and guidelines, with integrity and to the highest ethical standards **JOB SUMMARY:** BHFS operates at the intersection of global commerce and complex, multi\-jurisdictional financial regulation. We are seeking a \- CIO Regulated Payments to serve also as our designated Chief Information Officer (PCF\-49\) in Europe. This role carries a comprehensive global remit, providing technical and regulatory oversight across all jurisdictions in which BHFS operates. This is a technical governance and orchestration oversight role designed for a leader who excels at managing systemic scale through strategic partnership. You are the primary technical authority for BHFS, responsible for the integrity and scalability of our global regulated payments architecture. While this role leads a specialised internal BHFS team, it does not directly manage a large\-scale engineering organisation. Instead, you will lead the BHFS technical function and orchestrate global delivery through a high\-impact partnership model: * Primary Technology Partner: Booking.com Fintech \& Payments organisation. * Supplementary Partners: Booking Holdings central infrastructure teams (Cybersecurity, Data, AI, Enterprise Architecture) and Third\-Party Vendors. * Internal Oversight: Direct leadership of a dedicated BHFS team focused on technical governance, regulatory alignment, and partnership accountability. This role reports directly to the BHFS CEO, is a member of the Executive Leadership team in BHFS and a member of the Risk Committee where you will provide transparent assessments of technology risks, capabilities, and delivery. You will be expected to raise concerns early when partner delivery is at risk or regulatory compliance is at risk, recommend strategic technology investments and partnerships and represent BHFS technology interests in group\-level forums. This role is a pre\-approval controlled function as defined by the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 Regulations 2011\. Any appointment will be conditional on the Company being satisfied that the appointee meets the requirements as set out in the Fitness and Probity Standards issued by the Central Bank, obtains prior approval by the Central Bank of Ireland and ratification by the Company Board of Directors. This role requires the Company to complete prescribed due diligence to assess the appointee’s fitness and probity. **Key Responsibilities** Strategic Partnership: Advance the wider Booking Holdings growth strategy by leveraging BHFS’s regulated payments infrastructure in partnership with the global Fintech organisation, ensuring that all Fintech initiatives, where applicable, are built upon a compliant and resilient BHFS architecture. BHFS Evolution: Act as the primary BHFS technical stakeholder for next\-generation Fintech infrastructure, collaborating closely with intra\-group and Fintech teams to evaluate and support the integration of new payment rails and regulatory requirements. Architectural Oversight: Act as the final BHFS oversight lead for Enterprise Architecture. Ensure the Architecture supports high\-volume, low\-latency transaction processing and Financial Crime Risk Management (FCRM) integration across all jurisdictions. Digital Transformation: Lead BHFS stakeholders through complex digital transformation cycles, acting as the bridge between group\-level innovation and the specific requirements of a regulated payments environment. Technical Authority: Resolve technical PRD issues which arise between BHFS business requirements and intra\-group partner engineering constraints, ensuring readiness for PSD3 and other regulatory frameworks. FCRM Technical Strategy: Serve as a key BHFS stakeholder in the development of Financial Crime Risk Management (FCRM) tooling, partnering with Booking Holdings brands to ensure technical strategies align with business requirements.. Data Oversight \& AI Strategy: Partner with Booking Holdings brands to mature data management capabilities whilst establishing proportionate multi jurisdictional data governance principles and reporting capabilities . Act as the designated AI lead for BHFS; oversight of AI implementation in compliance with the EU AI Act and working with Booking Holdings brands to leverage AI tooling for efficiency gains Cybersecurity Leadership: Oversee all cybersecurity efforts in conjunction with BHFS CISO, ensuring robust protection of company data and systems against evolving global threats. Operational Resilience: Executive ownership for the technical components of the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) and Operational Resilience mandates, including first\-line ICT risk management, ICT resilience testing oversight, Crisis and Business Continuity Management and global Disaster Recovery Oversight and Planning. **Essential Requirements** * Payments Leadership in a Product/Tech\-led Environment: Proven track record of implementing regulated payments infrastructure within high\-growth, product\-led environments (e.g., global marketplaces or payments platforms). * Domain Expertise: Significant experience in high\-scale payments infrastructure specifically ensuring system reliability, transaction accuracy, and high\-performance delivery. * Global Regulatory Fluency: Practical knowledge of the interplay between technical systems and international regulations (AML Directives, GDPR, Data Residency, PSD3, DORA, Safeguarding). * Technical Orchestration: Proven ability to manage and hold intra\-group partners accountable to strict SLAs and technical standards. * Regulatory Standing: Must satisfy the Central Bank of Ireland’s Fitness and Probity Standards and be eligible for appointment as a Pre\-Approval Controlled Function (PCF\-49, Chief Information Officer). **Preferred Qualifications** * Prior PCF Experience: Previous experience holding a PCF role (e.g. CIO or equivalent) within an Electronic Money Institution (EMI) or Payment Institution (PI). * FCRM Implementation: Experience in the implementation and optimisation of high\-volume Financial Crime Risk Management (FCRM) tools, supporting transaction monitoring and AML controls at scale. **CORE COMPETENCIES** * Strategic Systems Thinking: Aligning complex financial technology initiatives with corporate vision. * Operational Control: A focus on efficiency, reliability, and automated governance. * High\-Agency Leadership: The ability to drive results through technical authority and partnership. * Cyber \& Risk Maturity: Deep understanding of the global threat landscape and ICT resilience. * Influence Energized by partnership and influence rather than frustrated by not having direct control. * Stakeholder Management Can navigate complex stakeholder landscapes and resolve conflicts constructively. * Credibility Bring both deep technical credibility AND the ability to translate technical decisions for non\-technical executives and regulators * Curiosity Intellectually curious about fintech, payments innovation, and emerging regulatory frameworks (AI, crypto, open banking) * Collaboration: Lead with humility and collaboration.

title: Cabin Crew Assessment Day – Dublin – 24 July 2026
description: **Description** Would you like to be part of the Ryanair Group’s amazing cabin crew family of 16k\+ crew? If you are customer\-oriented, love delivering great service \& want fast\-track career opportunities, we would be delighted to hear from you! No experience is required, just bags of enthusiasm \& team spirit! We are currently recruiting for Cabin Crew positions for one of our Dublin base and would be delighted to meet you at one of our In\-Person Recruitment Days! **Date: 24 July 2026** As Europe’s largest airline group carrying over 600K guests on over 3,600 daily flights, we are looking for the next generation of cabin crew to join us in the Autumn/Winter months at one of **our 90\+ operational bases** . Flying on board Ryanair Group aircraft there are some amazing perks, including; discounted staff travel to over 250\+ destinations across the Ryanair network, a fixed 5/3 roster pattern, free training \& industry\-leading pay. Your journey to becoming a qualified cabin crew member will start on a 6\-week training course where you will learn all of the fundamental skills that you will require as part of your day\-to\-day role delivering a top\-class safety \& customer service experience to our guests. During the course you will be required to study with exams taking place at regular intervals, the training culminates with Supernumerary flights, followed by your Cabin Crew Wings. As a member of the Ryanair Group cabin crew family, you will be immersed into our culture from day one, the career opportunities are endless including becoming a number 1, base supervisor, Regional Manager or why not aspire to becoming our Director of Inflight? Life as Cabin Crew is fun \& rewarding, it is however a demanding position where safety is our number 1 priority. You will be required to operate both early \& late shifts \& report for duty as early as 5 am in the morning on the early roster \& not return home until midnight on the afternoon roster. If you are not a morning person, then think twice before applying! **Some of our amazing benefits;** * Free Internationally recognised Cabin Crew Training Course * Daily per Diem whilst training – €28 per day * Paid Training * New Joiners Bonus of €2000 * Uncapped Sales bonus * Unlimited highly discounted Staff Travel * Fixed 5 days on / 3 days off roster pattern * The adventure and experience of a lifetime within our Cabin Crew network * Explore new cultures and cities with colleagues on your days off * Free Uniform in Year 1 and annual allowance afterwards * Security of working for a financially stable Airline **Requirements** * Applicants must hold the unrestricted right to live and work in the EU and travel freely throughout the Ryanair Network * You must approximately be between 5 “2 (157 cm) and 6” 2 (188 cm) in height. * You must be able to swim 25 meters unaided. * It helps if you are hardworking, flexible and have an outgoing and friendly personality. * Adaptable and happy to work a shift roster. * Enjoy dealing with the public and have the ability to provide excellent customer service with a ‘can do’ attitude. * Comfortable speaking and writing in English with ease. * A passion for travelling and meeting new people.

title: Chemistry Laboratory Technician
description: **SUMMARY OF ROLE:** Performing routine chemical testing on a variety of food and wastewater matrices using various analytical wet chemistry and instrumental methods. Assisting with method development, commissioning and validation projects. Performing daily checks, calibrations, record keeping and other duties to support laboratory testing. **Applications are welcomed from both experienced and graduate candidates as we have positions at various levels dependent on experience** **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:** 1\. Perform routine chemical testing of food matrices for a variety of nutritional and allergen test methods 2\. Perform routine chemical testing of wastewaters and waters for a range of environmental chemical testing 3\. Assist with maintenance of laboratory equipment and supplies 4\. Assist with equipment installation and qualification 5\. Perform daily equipment checks as required by QMS including check on balances, pipette and temperature monitoring of equipment 6\. Participate in QC checks to ensure compliance with quality requirements 7\. Assist with method development, commissioning of equipment and validation trials 8\. Ensure laboratory records are completed correctly and promptly to ensure adequate traceability 9\. Assist with training of other colleagues 10\. To abide by all company policies and procedures 11\. To work in accordance with the Quality Management System (QMS) and Health and Safety guidelines 12\. To inform Line Manager of any apparent problems/doubts while undertaking work delegated 13\. To record and manage all data accurately and in the specified format (i.e., written and/or electronically) 14\. To ensure all records are fully completed, legible and stored appropriately for traceability 15\. To ensure your Training Record is current and reflective of training completed 16\. To participate in investigations of Complaints and Non\-Conforming Work as required 17\. To participate in both Internal and External Audits as required 18\. To ensure the appropriate PPE is used at all times whilst carrying out tasks as required 19\. To ensure proper management of stock / consumables required for completion of work 20\. To ensure that your designated work area is kept clean and tidy at all times **QUALIFICATIONS \& EXPERIENCE** * Third level qualification in Food Science or Analytical Chemistry is preferred * Minimum of 2 years relevant practical experience required for more senior roles * Working knowledge of laboratory practices particularly analytical chemistry techniques is desirable but full training will be provided for entry level positions * Fluent in English and confident with record keeping * Good organisational and IT skills and ability to plan and manage own workload * Good troubleshooting and analytical skills * Able to work alone and within a team. Flexible and adaptive to changes in business requirements * Willingness to learn and develop * Task focussed working under agreed deadlines, technically minded with good attention to detail **\<\<\< Applications are welcomed from both experienced and graduate candidates as we have positions at various levels dependent on experience \>\>\>** **Method application:** Candidates meeting specified criteria are asked to please submit CV and covering letter to careers@premierlifesciences.ie quoting reference LT04\#0626 and to include your current salary, notice period and availability for interview. We look forward to receiving your application. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €29,500\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Ballon, County Carlow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chief Commercial Officer
description: Brightwater Executive is delighted to be retained exclusively by Moneycorp Technologies Ltd (MTL), for the search of their Chief Commercial Officer. **About the Company** Moneycorp Technologies Limited (MTL) is a Dublin\-headquartered payments and foreign exchange business, empowering corporate and private clients to move money and manage currency risk across borders. Established in Ireland in 2013, MTL operates across Ireland, France and Spain, primarily serving corporate markets with tailored FX risk management and international payment solutions. Backed by deep market expertise and a strong client\-centric culture, we help our clients navigate global complexity with confidence. Moneycorp powers global payments for corporates, institutions and high net worth individuals, combining worldwide reach with local expertise. With over 45 years of market experience, two banking licences and offices in 11 countries, their technology delivers 24/7 accessibility, while their people deliver award\-winning service. In 2024, they supported £79bn\+ of international payments, reaching 190 countries in 130 currencies. **About This Role:** The Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will be responsible for commercial execution, payments capability and delivery of strategic change across the MTL franchise. The role has a broad set of commercial and operational responsibilities requiring specific expertise in payments, SEPA Instant, and change management at a critical point in the firm’s evolution. ***This is a hands\-on leadership role, combining commercial ownership, operational problem\-solving, and transformation delivery, supporting both near\-term execution and longer\-term strategic positioning in payments and solutions.*** **Key Responsibilities:** **Commercial And Revenue Leadership** * Own and govern the corporate sales and pipeline management framework. * Lead monthly commercial performance reviews across Dublin, Madrid, and Paris. * Partner with sales \& relationship teams to drive disciplined execution and conversion. * Act as senior commercial escalation point, for complex client, pricing and deliverables. **Payments and SEPA Instant Leadership:** * Serve as the senior business lead for SEPA Instant readiness and execution, working across Technology, Operations, Compliance and Commercial teams. * Ensure payments requirements are embedded into: Commercial propositions; Client experience design and Operational processes * Bring practical payments expertise (instant payments, flow monitoring, exception handling, settlement considerations) into day\-to\-day decision\-making. * Support the broader evolution of the business into payments and payment\-adjacent solutions. **Transformation and Change Management:** * Act as MTL’s business owner for technology and operational transformation initiatives. * Drive structured change delivery * Translate regulatory and technology change into executable business actions. **COO \& Cross Functional Leadership Team Collaboration:** * Provide day to day senior operational support to the COO. * Act as first line escalation for cross functional commercial and operational issues, helping to embed pragmatic operational improvements across the franchise. * Work closely with Compliance as a business counterpart, supporting implementation led regulatory change and prudent operational enhancements. * Partner with Finance on commercially relevant regulatory returns (e.g. CESOP) and act as a connective leader across Trading, Commercial, Operations, Finance and Compliance **Key Skills \& Leadership Experience:** * 9 – 15 years \+ senior commercial leadership experience within regulated financial services, payments, or regulated financial institutions. * Demonstrable payments experience, ideally including: SEPA / SEPA Instant; Transactional flows; Operational payments processes. * Commercially astute with strong execution discipline and highly credible leader. * Pragmatic, delivery oriented, and solutions focused. * A natural integrator who can translate strategy into action. Comfortable operating in ambiguity and evolving environments. * Proven involvement in business led transformation or change programmes, particularly technology or regulatory driven change. * Strong cross functional operating experience across commercial, operations, compliance and technology teams. * Comfortable operating in a regulated environment and working alongside other SLT / PCF team members * Ideally experienced supporting or delivering payments platform implementations or upgrades across multi\-jurisdictional operations. * Sound background in FX, payments, transaction banking, or payment services providers. Brightwater Executive is delighted to be retained exclusively by MTL for this Chief Commercial Officer role opening. To request a more detailed job spec and / or for all queries, please connect for a confidential conversation with Estelle Davis at Brightwater Executive on e.davis@brightwaterexecutive.com or call on DD \+ 353 1 5927 844\.

title: Chief Executive Officer

title: Chief Technology Officer
description: **Chief Technology Officer (CTO)** Location: Dublin, Ireland (on\-site; relocation required if not currently based in Dublin) Reporting to: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) **About Foodhub** Foodhub is a fast\-growing global technology company entering an exciting new phase of expansion. With a newly established Dublin headquarters and operations across the UK, USA, ANZ and India, we are building a high\-calibre senior leadership team to help shape the future of the business. This is a rare opportunity to join at a pivotal stage — the energy and ambition of a scale\-up, backed by the stability and success of an established international business. Foodhub is profitable, debt\-free, and has delivered sustained year\-on\-year growth across multiple international markets. As we accelerate our global expansion strategy through both organic growth and international acquisitions, we are looking for ambitious leaders who want to play a defining role in building something significant. This is more than a leadership position — it’s an opportunity to influence the direction of a dynamic global business at the start of its next major growth chapter. **Overview** Foodhub is seeking a highly experienced and visionary Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our global technology strategy, platform development, and engineering organisation. This is a critical executive role responsible for scaling Foodhub’s technology infrastructure, driving innovation, and ensuring the delivery of secure, reliable, and high\-performing products across all markets. The CTO will play a key role in shaping the company’s long\-term technical vision while ensuring operational excellence in day\-to\-day technology delivery. Working closely with the CEO and executive leadership team, the CTO will lead a global engineering and technology function, ensuring alignment between technology, product, and business objectives. This role will involve **regular international travel** across Foodhub’s global markets to support teams, align strategy, and drive execution. **Key Responsibilities** **Technology Strategy \& Leadership** * Define and execute Foodhub’s global technology strategy aligned with business goals and growth plans. * Partner with the CEO and executive team to shape long\-term company strategy and innovation roadmap. * Ensure technology capabilities support scalability, reliability, and competitive advantage. * Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging technologies to drive innovation and differentiation. **Platform \& Product Delivery** * Oversee the design, development, and delivery of Foodhub’s core platform, products, and services. * Ensure high\-quality, scalable, and resilient systems that support global operations. * Drive best practices in software development, architecture, and engineering excellence. * Work closely with product and commercial teams to deliver customer\-centric solutions. **Engineering \& Team Leadership** * Build, lead, and scale a high\-performing global engineering and technology organisation. * Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. * Develop leadership capability within engineering teams across multiple regions. * Attract, retain, and develop top technical talent. **Infrastructure, Security \& Reliability** * Oversee global technology infrastructure, ensuring performance, scalability, and uptime. * Ensure robust cybersecurity, data protection, and compliance standards across all markets. * Implement best\-in\-class DevOps, cloud, and system monitoring practices. * Manage risk and ensure business continuity through strong technical governance. **Data \& Technology Enablement** * Champion data\-driven decision\-making across the organisation. * Oversee data architecture, analytics capabilities, and platform integrations. * Drive the use of automation, AI, and emerging technologies to improve efficiency and customer experience. **Process, Systems \& Transformation** * Establish scalable development processes, tools, and frameworks. * Lead technology transformation initiatives to support business growth and efficiency. * Ensure alignment between global teams, systems, and operational requirements. **Financial \& Vendor Management** * Manage technology budgets, ensuring efficient investment and strong return on spend. * Evaluate and manage third\-party vendors, platforms, and partnerships. * Identify opportunities to optimise costs and improve technology ROI. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Proven experience as a CTO or in a senior technology leadership role within a large\-scale, global organisation. * **Direct experience within the food ordering, hospitality technology, marketplace, or POS/EPOS industry is highly desirable.** * Strong background in building and scaling high\-availability, customer\-facing platforms. * Experience leading large, distributed engineering teams across multiple regions. * Deep technical expertise in modern software architecture, cloud infrastructure, and scalable systems. * Demonstrated success in driving technology transformation and innovation. * Strong understanding of cybersecurity, data protection, and regulatory compliance. * Experience aligning technology strategy with commercial and operational objectives. * Excellent stakeholder management and ability to influence at executive and board level. * Degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience). **Key Skills \& Attributes** * Strategic thinker with strong technical depth and execution capability. * Innovative and forward\-looking with a passion for technology and product excellence. * Strong leadership and team\-building capabilities. * Highly analytical with strong problem\-solving skills. * Results\-driven with a focus on delivery, quality, and scalability. * Excellent communication skills, able to translate technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders. * Resilient and adaptable in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment. * Culturally aware with experience working across global markets. * Willingness and ability to travel internationally on a regular basis. **Benefits** * Competitive compensation and executive benefits package. * Opportunity to lead and scale the technology function of a global platform business. * Direct partnership with CEO and executive leadership team. * Dynamic and collaborative work environment with significant impact and ownership. * 33 days annual leave per year (including public holidays). * Option to buy up to 5 additional annual leave days per year. * Guaranteed day off on your birthday (when requested). * Option to purchase Bupa Select Complete Health Insurance (Medical History Disregarded) for self and family at a significantly discounted rate. * €35 monthly gym contribution. Pay: €200,000\.00\-€400,000\.00 per year Application question(s): * How do you currently use AI tools to improve your effectiveness as a technology leader? Please provide specific examples of how AI has influenced engineering productivity, product development, decision\-making, or operational efficiency. * Describe your experience working directly with founders or executive leadership teams. What was your role in shaping technology strategy, company growth, and key business decisions? * How many years of engineering leadership experience do you have, and what is the largest team (or organisation) you have directly managed? Please include the number of engineering managers and individual contributors within your scope. * How hands\-on are you currently with software architecture, code reviews, and technical design decisions? Work Location: In person
