title: Facilities Assistant
description: **Job Description** **Exceptional Hospitality Starts with You** Picture yourself brightening someone’s day. When you join our Hotels team, that’s exactly what you’ll do as a Facilities Assistant, you’re not just helping maintain the hotel’s physical safety and functionality – you’re spreading the light and warmth of hospitality by delivering memorable experiences that make the stay for every guest. **Responsibilities** Here's what you'll do during a typical day: * Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas: Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas * Respond to service requests: Quickly handle guest calls and team member work orders * Support a variety of maintenance tasks: Conduct repairs and maintenance across various areas, including carpentry, plumbing, electrical, HVAC and masonry * Assist with preventive maintenance inspections: Inspect facilities and equipment to identify and address preventive maintenance needs * Document maintenance activities: Record completed inspections and repairs and flag any issues requiring further attention **Qualifications** **What It Takes to Make the Stay** At Hilton, Our Core Values Define What It Takes To Succeed Here And Guide The Qualities We Look For In Every Team Member A passion for spreading the light and warmth of **Hospitality** . Acting with **Integrity** and always doing the right thing. Inspiring others through **Leadership** . A belief that **Teamwork** drives the best outcomes. A sense of **Ownership** and accountability. And a focus on the **Now** , bringing urgency and discipline to every moment, knowing it can make a lasting impact. **About Us** **Join an Award\-Winning Workplace Culture** At Hilton, we don’t just deliver exceptional experiences for our guests—we build an exceptional workplace for the Team Members who make it all possible. As a global leader in hospitality, we’ve welcomed more than 3 billion guests worldwide, all while staying true to our founding vision: to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality. Our award\-winning culture has earned us repeated recognition on the World’s Best Workplaces list by Great Place to Work and Fortune. With our suite of world\-class brands , and a company\-wide commitment to providing the best stay for every guest, we’re setting new standards for the future of travel. Whether you’re starting your career or exploring something new, Hilton supports your journey every step of the way. Come for the job, stay for the career—and help us make every stay a little more magical. Curious about life at Hilton? Explore our Careers Blog to see why we’re more than a great place to stay—we’re a great place to work. **About The Team** **How We’ll Help You Thrive** **Benefits** At Hilton, the hospitality we’re known for doesn’t end with our guests. We proudly invest in our Team Members’ wellbeing, supporting you through all of life’s moments. When you join Hilton, our exceptional care extends to you with unmatched perks and benefits, including\*: * Incredible travel perks – Enjoy 110 nights of deeply discounted travel, with room rates as low as $40 USD/night at our world\-class hotels through our Go Hilton travel program * Own a piece of Hilton – Through our employee stock purchase program (ESPP), you can invest in Hilton shares at a 15% discount to help build your financial future * Paid parental leave – Because family matters. We offer paid leave for eligible Team Members, including partners and adoptive parents * Personalized caregiving support – Access dedicated caregiving concierge services to help navigate caregiving for yourself and your loved ones * Crisis concierge – In times of loss, our Crisis Concierge offers a single, compassionate point of contact for both practical support and emotional care * Mental health resources – Your wellbeing comes first. Through our Care for All hub, we provide resources to help our Team Members to care for themselves, care for members of their team, and care for their loved ones. Eligible Team Members receive free counseling and support through our best\-in\-class Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * Benefits availability may vary depending on Team Member’s location as well as terms and conditions of employment and are subject to the terms and conditions of each specific program/plan.

title: Facilities Coordinator
description: **Job Title** Facilities Coordinator **Job Description Summary** The Facilities Coordinator leads and supports the day\-to\-day operations of Sony Interactive Entertainment’s (SIE) Dublin office, delivering a high\-performing, safe, and seamlessly run workplace environment. This role blends hands\-on facilities coordination with strategic workplace management — owning ticket management, vendor relationships, health and safety compliance, space planning, and employee experience. The ideal candidate is a proactive, detail\-oriented workplace professional who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and is equally comfortable working independently and cross\- functionally. **Job Description** **Core Responsibilities** * Workplace \& Facilities Operations * Lead day\-to\-day facilities operations to ensure a safe, functional, and well\-maintained workplace * Act as the primary workplace contact for SIE employees, fostering an environment * Conduct structured facilities and space readiness walks to proactively identify and resolve deficiencies. * Conduct routine and monthly site inspections to ensure the site meets service quality and operational standards. * Ensure meeting rooms, collaboration spaces, and common meet readiness and presentation standards. * Manage all aspects of health and safety for the Dublin offices, ensuring full compliance with applicable regulations and SIE standards. * Support preventative maintenance program tracking and coordination of vendor services (janitorial, maintenance, specialty trades) * Collaborate with building management, local leadership, and cross\-functional teams to maintain operational efficiency aligned with SIE standards. * Ticket Management * Serve as the primary owner for facilities ticket management at the regional sites. * Monitor incoming requests, assign work orders as appropriate, and ensure timely follow through. * Track open and closed tickets and escalate issues that require additional attention. * Maintain strong service delivery by monitoring SLAs, response times, and completion timelines. * Support reporting on ticket volume, trends, KPIs, and service performance metrics. * Communication and Request Intake * Serve as the primary point of contact for facilities\-related Slack channel communications. * Serve as the primary owner of the facilities email alias, including monitoring, routing, and responding to requests. * Gather and act on employee feedback to implement improvements, sharing insights and larger\-scale opportunities with Workplace leadership. * Maintain clear and professional written communication with employees, vendors, and internal stakeholders. * Act as a go\-to resource for employees, addressing facilities\-related inquiries and requests. * Event \& Workplace Experience Support * Support planning and execution of onsite events and employee programming, including coordination of logistics (catering, A/V, space setup, vendor access). * Ensure event space readiness through setup, walkthroughs, and alignment with Facilities, IT/AV, and vendor partners. * Provide day\-of event support as a visible point of contact, troubleshooting issues to ensure a seamless experience. * Assist with post\-event breakdown, space reset, and continuous improvement based on feedback. * Vendor \& Contract Management * Build and manage strong relationships with external vendors and suppliers, ensuring performance aligns with SLAs in collaboration with the Vendor Enablement team. * Monitor vendor safety performance, conduct regular reviews, and proactively address any issues or gaps. * Provide vendor escorting in accordance with site access and safety procedures. * Support maintenance and renewal of service contracts, including documentation and compliance tracking. * Coordinate service schedules, work status updates, and follow\-up communications with vendors and internal teams. * Financial \& Administrative Support * Create and manage Purchase Orders (POs) in alignment with budget and contract requirements. * Process and track vendor invoices, ensuring accuracy and timely payment. * Partner with Facilities Managers to support monthly accruals, including tracking open POs and outstanding expenses. * Assist in budget tracking, variance reporting, and maintaining accurate financial records. * Support vendor onboarding and ensure compliance with contractual and financial requirements. * Create and maintain clear written documentation related to service requests, work orders, and issue resolution. **Skills And Qualifications** * 5\+ years of experience in facilities management, workplace services, or a closely related field * Experience working with Integrated Facilities Management (IFM) contractors and suppliers and monitoring service standards; solid understanding of compliance requirements. * Strong work ethic with the ability to take initiative and function in a fast\-paced working environment with immediate and changing deadlines. * Strong problem solving and decision\-making skills; strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and time management are a must. * Demonstrated strong and effective verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills; ability to interact comfortably and effectively with managers and employees at all levels within the organization. * Proficiency in facilities management platforms, ticketing systems, and Microsoft Office / Google Workspace tools; comfortable using Slack as a primary communication channel. **KPIs / Success Measures** * Ticket response time and SLA compliance * Timely resolution of facilities and workplace requests * Email alias and Slack channel responsiveness * Vendor performance outcomes and contract compliance * Quality of stakeholder communication and employee satisfaction with workplace services * Accuracy and timeliness of financial administration, including POs, invoices, and accruals * Successful delivery of space planning projects, MAC activities, and workplace enhancements * Health and safety compliance and audit readiness INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Fashion Brand & Sales Development Assistant
description: **Fashion Brand \& Sales Development Assistant** ​**Company**: Silk \& Sequins **Location**: Carlow Town, Co. Carlow (Remote\-First / Hybrid) **Job Type:** Part\-Time, Fixed\-Term (4\-Week Initial Trial ) **Hours**: 5 hours per week (Highly flexible scheduling) **Salary**: €14\.15 per hour (\+ 8% holiday pay) \+ 10% commission on all successful retail accounts secured. ​**About Silk \& Sequins** ​Silk \& Sequins is an established fashion house transitioning its signature heritage of bespoke, high\-end tailoring into an exclusive, ultra\-luxury **Ready to Wear** line. Driven by high\-octane glamour, structural drama, and unapologetic femininity, our collections focus on premium garment architecture and show\-stopping evening wear. ​As we scale, we are launching a targeted wholesale strategy to place our pieces into premium, independent luxury retail boutiques across Ireland and Europe. ***We are seeking a sharp, stylish, and self\-motivated assistant to act as the commercial engine behind this launch.*** ​**The Role \& Future Opportunity** ​This is a **micro\-part\-time, remote\-first role** (5 hours per week) designed for an ambitious fashion/marketing student, recent graduate, or freelance professional who wants to build an incredible portfolio. ​**We are explicitly looking for a long\-term partner to grow with the brand.** To ensure a perfect mutual fit, the role will begin with an initial 4\-week trial period. Upon successful completion, this position is intended to transition into a permanent, long\-term role as our retail network expands. ​You will work directly with the Brand Founder to manage B2B outreach and elevate our brand presence. This role is a perfect blend of digital research, creative luxury marketing, and real\-world commercial pitches. ​**Key Responsibilities:** ​**Wholesale Market Research:** Identify, vet, and map out high\-end independent boutiques across Ireland that cater to premium occasion wear and luxury evening wear clientele. ​**B2B Sales Outreach:** Build and manage structured Excel/Google Sheet lead trackers, drafting and executing sophisticated pitch emails to retail buyers. ​**In\-Person Boutique Pitching:** Visit target luxury boutiques—either alongside the Brand Founder or independently—to present the collection, display the garment architecture, and secure wholesale accounts. ​**Brand \& Content Alignment:** Collaborate on creative ideas for our social media channels to ensure our digital presence looks pristine, high\-end, and highly attractive to boutique owners. ​**Studio Alignment (Occasional)**: Avail of flexible, periodic visits to our Carlow studio to review new garment architecture, understand the craftsmanship, and capture authentic behind\-the\-scenes content. ​**Requirements**: ​A profound understanding of, and passion for, the luxury fashion industry (evening wear, premium tailoring, and high\-end design). ​Confident, professional communication skills with the ability to pitch to retail buyers face\-to\-face and via email. ​High organizational discipline—you must be comfortable managing spreadsheets and tracking leads accurately. ​Familiarity with digital content tools (e.g., Canva, CapCut, social media editing) is a strong advantage. ​Based in or within reasonable commuting distance of Co. Carlow for occasional studio alignment and regional boutique visits. ​**Benefits**:A direct 10% commission on the wholesale value of all successful accounts you secure. ​**Long\-Term Career Growth:** The opportunity to establish yourself early within a growing luxury brand with a view to long\-term employment. ​**Ultimate Flexibility:** Choose your own working hours weekly to easily fit around your college classes or other freelance projects. ​**Direct Mentorship:** Work one\-on\-one with the fashion designer and founder, gaining rare, face\-to\-face experience in luxury retail placement. ​**To Apply:** ​Please submit your CV along with a brief note detailing your passion for the luxury fashion industry and why you are drawn to the world of high\-glamour evening wear. ​Applications close within 7 days. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a brief 15\-minute introductory video call. Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * Flexitime * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Carlow, County Carlow

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **About Ethos** Ethos is a Greek word meaning "character". It is used to describe the guiding beliefs or ideals that characterise a community, nation, or ideology. Ethos are mechanical and electrical consultants to the data centre, commercial, high\-tech healthcare, and pharmaceutical sectors. Our mission is to engineer systems that complement the purpose of each building we design, improve the immediate and surrounding community, and meet the objectives of its operations, inhabitants, patrons, operators, and staff. We are an ambitious, innovative, and driven. We are passionate about delivering excellence for our clients and their projects, and we never settle for delivering work quickly at the cost of quality. Our work environment is positive, professional, and supportive. We take our work seriously, but we like to have a lot of fun too and we place huge importance on having a great company culture. We have been recognised as a Great Place to Work in 2019\-2020 and in 2020\-2021 and were awarded Best Company to Work For 2022\. **About the role:** Ethos prides itself on delivering efficient, effective solutions across a wide range of industries. We’re currently searching for a qualified accountant to join our ranks as a Finance Business Partner and continue our tradition of success. This person will play an important role across all areas of finance for the entity, as well as supporting the FP\&A and Operations teams and other Ad hoc tasks as necessary. **Role Specification** **Candidate Attributes** * The candidate must be based in Ireland to be consider for this position * 2\-3 years post qualified experience (PQE) essential * Ideally working in an industry role in construction or service provider * Experience working in the UK market desirable but not essential * Strong commercial acumen * Good organisational skills, able to prioritise effectively \& escalate as necessary * Proven track record of strong work ethic. Clear examples of “rolling up sleeves and getting stuck in” * Proven track record of problem solving with clear examples * Proactive \& effective communicator, able to communicate both internally within the business and externally to clients \& customers * Team player, ability to work both independently and collaboratively with other team members, in pursuit of finance objectives * Strong IT skills, including a proficiency in the use of excel. Experience working with financial systems / ERP a must * Continuous improvement mindset **Your Day\-to\-Day Activities:** * Own the role as finance business partner and point of contact for all finance related matters for Ethos’ UK based subsidiary. You will be required to present analysis, provide insight and make recommendations for future strategies * Assist commercial team with pricing, invoicing and cash collection * Own the accounts payable process implementing a purchase order process and keeping creditor balances within terms * Own cash flow management for the company reporting to the financial controller and managing director of the business on a weekly and monthly basis * Own the payroll process working with HR and external payroll provider to ensure accurate processing and payment of payroll on monthly basis * Take ownership of the company’s ERP system maintaining accurate master data and ensuring adequate training and resourcing is provided to the rest of the team * Play an active role in month end including: * Production of company management accounts within group deadlines * Ensure all areas are prepared to a high degree of accuracy * Work with the Financial Controller and other finance team members on timely delivery of reporting deliverables * Manage general ledger postings, reconciliations and journal entries * Work with local compliance partners of foreign subsidiaries around tax and statutory filings. * Take ownership of balance sheet reconciliations and ensure all completed accurately and on time * Support the annual audit process, liaising with external auditors and preparing necessary schedules. * Maintain and improve internal controls, ensuring adherence to company policies * Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. * Provide financial analysis and reporting to support strategic and operational decisions. * Play an active role in new ERP system implementation * Occasional travel to the UK will be required * Assist financial controller and financial accountants with group reporting requirements * Assist Financial Controller and CFO with any ad hoc tasks. **Why Join us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A state\-of\-the\-art office in Charlemont, Dublin 2, designed to promote collaboration, innovation, and wellbeing. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times (in agreement with your Direct Manager) * Pension Scheme * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. Our vision is to: “ **Be the company people want to work with, and for** ” Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be EPIC \- for our people and our customers.

title: Finance Supervisor
description: **Finance Supervisor – Ballsbridge, Dublin 4** * **Location:** Ballsbridge, Dublin 4 (Excellent public transport links) * **Salary:** €45,000 \- €50,000 (DOE) * **Package:** Competitive Benefits \+ Rapid career growth potential * **Reporting to:** Financial Controller **About the Opportunity:** Looking for a career accelerator in hospitality finance? This is an exciting opportunity for an ambitious finance professional to join the team at the heart of Ballsbridge as a **Finance Supervisor** . This is the perfect stepping\-stone for a Senior Accounts Assistant or Assistant Accountant looking to take the next step into leadership. You will enjoy a highly visible role with a fast\-tracked route into a **Finance Manager** position, gaining exposure to high\-level month\-end and forecasting from day one. **The Role:** * **Team Leadership:** Supervise, mentor, and develop a small on\-site team (directly managing the Accounts Assistant and Graduate). * **Financial Ownership:** Take day\-to\-day responsibility for managing Accounts Receivable (AR), Accounts Payable (AP), and Income Audit processes. * **Month\-End \& Strategy:** Support the Financial Controller with Month\-End closure, forecasting, and driving financial systems efficiency. **What We Are Looking For:** * **Skills:** Strong working knowledge of day\-to\-day finance operations (AP/AR/Income Audit). Intermediate Excel skills and a tech\-savvy approach to finance systems are preferred. * **Mindset:** An organised, proactive individual ready to roll up their sleeves, take ownership, and lead junior team members.

title: Float Secretary
description: ### **Description** DAC Beachcroft Dublin is looking to recruit a Float Legal Secretary to work within the office assisting teams in general. We are seeking a secretary who is accustomed to working in a busy environment. The role will be office based with working hours of Monday to Friday 10am to 6pm and full training will be given. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Preparing correspondence and documents from digital dictation * Preparing briefs * Creating, amending and formatting legal documents and templates with a high degree of accuracy * Updating the MI databases * File management, including/both physical and electronic filing, scanning, photocopying and archiving * Liaising with experts/counsel * Arranging meetings/consultations * Diary management * Assist with expense claims * Booking of meeting rooms * Issuing large documents such as briefs to Counsel/experts via OneDrive * Billing preparation and issuing bills to clients * Debt collection * Booking travel ### **Skills, Knowledge \& Expertise** * Proven experience of working as a Legal Secretary however full training will be given * Strong dictation experience * A minimum of 60 wpm * Proficient use of Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, PDF Docs and OneDrive * Strong attention to detail and accuracy is essential * Excellent administration skills * Ability to work on one's own initiative * Ability to draft correspondence when asked to do so * Excellent organisational skills * Ability to prioritise large workloads * Ability to multi\-task and work to strict deadlines * Team orientated and collaborative with a flexible attitude * Professional telephone manner and excellent communication skills, both written and oral * The candidate should have a positive, helpful and cheerful attitude * Experience of preparing briefs ### **About DAC Beachcroft** We are a leading international legal business with over 3,000 colleagues and a diverse range of capabilities. We are market leaders in insurance, health and real estate but with a full\-service offering, including growing Financial Services and Tech capabilities. We represent a wide range of clients – from household names to government departments and NHS bodies. We act for nearly all of the top\-20 UK and global insurers, we're market leaders in the provision of strategic, commercial and regulatory advice to providers and commissioners in the health sector, and in real estate we advise all of the UK’s top\-five housebuilders. We are a fast\-growing international business with offices around the globe. In addition to our 11 UK offices, we also have offices in Bogota, Buenos Aires, Dublin, Lima, Los Angeles, Hong Kong, Madrid, Mexico City, Milan, New York, Paris, Rome, Santiago de Chile, and Singapore plus a representative office in Miami and broad network of affiliations across the globe. At DAC Beachcroft we are committed to and recognise the importance of equity, diversity and inclusion across the board, including and beyond the protected characteristics. We take decisions based solely on merit. We value, welcome and seek out people with different backgrounds and life experiences, perspectives, beliefs, ways of thinking and identities. We are happy to talk flexible working with our Flex Forward scheme. We would encourage you to talk to us about our approach to flexible working during the hiring process if you would like to explore this further. **Note for Recruitment Agencies** DAC Beachcroft manages all vacancies via our in\-house recruitment teams, prioritising direct sourcing and referrals. When external support is required, roles are released to selected agencies on our Preferred Supplier List (PSL). Speculative CVs sent to any DAC Beachcroft employee without prior instruction from our recruitment teams (LLP and CSG) will not be accepted, and no fees will be payable. For PSL queries, please contact: recruitment@dacbeachcroft.com or csgrecruitment@dacbeachcroft.com

title: Founding Engineer
description: Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. We're hiring our first in\-house engineer. Until now our platform has been built with an external development partner; you'll bring it in\-house, own it, and set the technical direction from here. This is a founding role in the real sense your decisions on architecture, tooling and engineering culture will stick. Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. Tasks * Own the Positive Carbon platform end to end: the food\-waste capture pipeline, the data and analytics layer, and the dashboards customers use to find and reduce wasted\-food cost. * Turn raw measurement into financial insight waste value by site, category and meal period. * Build AI into the product as a first\-class capability, not a bolt\-on: agents and LLM pipelines for enrichment, reporting, anomaly detection and operational recommendations. * Use AI aggressively in how you build. We expect a small team to ship like a much larger one. * Stand up the engineering foundations we'll need to scale: CI/CD, observability, data quality and a sane release process. * Transition us cleanly off the agency setup without disrupting live customers. Requirements * Startup or founding experience (must\-have): you've worked at an early\-stage company or in a founding/first\-hire role and you're at home with ambiguity, ownership and a blank page. * Strong full\-stack experience across backend, frontend and data and the instinct to go deep wherever the hard problem is. * A track record of shipping production software that real customers rely on. * Genuine fluency with modern AI tooling you build with LLMs and agents and have opinions on where they help and where they don't. * Pragmatism over perfection: you scope hard, ship, and iterate. * Comfort being the technical owner when there's no one else to ask. Benefits * Health Insurance * Pension * Bike to work * 25 Days Annual Leave * Hybrid Work * Computer vision, IoT/edge or data\-pipeline experience. * Experience as a founding engineer or very early hire. * Interest in the climate and food\-systems problem. **Send a short note on why this role, plus a CV or LinkedIn, or through our website. Tell us about something you built, shipped or closed not just where you worked.**

title: Freelance Videographer – Sports Club Events
description: Join a dynamic environment at a newly established Padel Club in Dublin 4, where you will play a key role in capturing the vibrant atmosphere, facilities, and events that define the club experience. This position is ideal for a creative videographer who is passionate about sports and storytelling through visual media. You will be responsible for producing high\-quality video content that highlights the club’s unique offerings and helps build its brand presence. **Deliverables** * Capture on\-site video footage of club facilities, matches, and special events in Dublin 4\. * Edit and deliver polished video content suitable for promotional use across digital platforms. * Collaborate with club management to understand key messaging and visual style requirements. * Ensure all video content aligns with the club’s branding and quality standards. * Manage video files, backups, and delivery timelines efficiently. **Requirements** * Proven experience in videography, preferably in sports or event settings. * Proficiency with professional video cameras, lighting, and audio equipment. * Strong video editing skills using industry\-standard software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro). * Ability to work independently on\-site in Dublin 4 and adapt to a fast\-paced club environment. * Excellent communication and organizational skills. * Creative eye for detail and storytelling. * Availability for on\-site shoots, including evenings or weekends as required. **About Twine** Twine is a leading freelance marketplace connecting top freelancers, consultants, and contractors with companies needing creative and tech expertise. Trusted by Fortune 500 companies and innovative startups alike, Twine enables companies to scale their teams globally. **Our Mission** Twine's mission is to empower creators and businesses to thrive in an AI\-driven, freelance\-first world.

title: Full Price - IRL Sales Team Member
description: Why join us? We’re more than just a shoe brand—we have been at the forefront of innovative shoemaking since its foundation in 1825\. That’s right! 200 years! If you have a great attitude, love working with people, we want to hear from you! **About the role:** We are looking for a reliable and enthusiastic Sales Associate to provide a great consumer experience in store whilst delivering set targets and KPIs. Effectively selling shoes and accessories through the delivery of the appropriate Clarks selling models. Support in the day\-to\-day maintenance of high retail standards both front and back of house. **We’re looking for someone who will:** * Consistently deliver a great brand experience, including utilising technology to enhance the consumers’ experience and keeping up to date with product knowledge. * Support the achievement of key store KPI’s by delivering sales at target levels set by store Management team. * Maintain the sales floor and back of house areas in order to deliver high brand and retail standards * Flexibility to work a 7\-day shift pattern, including weekends and bank holidays. * Follow cash and stock security procedures to Company standards. * Maintain visual merchandising standards and replenish as required. Benefits We reward our Sales Associates with a comprehensive package, including: * Retail Trust * Life Assurance * Clarks Discount Card * Holiday Pay * Eyecare Vouchers * Clarks Workplace Pension Scheme * Potential to win store bonus if overachieve set KPI’s Join us and bring your positive energy to Clarks – where every day is an adventure!

title: Full Stack Developer
description: **Virtu** Virtu is a leading financial firm that leverages cutting edge technology to deliver liquidity to the global markets and innovative, transparent trading solutions to our clients. As a market maker, Virtu provides deep liquidity that helps to create more efficient markets around the world. Our market structure expertise, broad diversification, and execution technology enables us to provide competitive bids and offers in over 19,000 securities, at over 235 venues, in 36 countries worldwide **THE ROLE** We are looking for an exceptional candidate to join our development team. Our team is a talented group of versatile software engineers who collaborate with the entire Virtu group to build the next generation of web\-based trading tools for our fast paced firm. Members of this team collaborate closely with traders, operations, and other developers to gather and understand needs, and implement real\-time trading, visualization, analysis and reporting tools. Ideal candidates will have a passion and talent for designing great, intuitive user interfaces as well as scalable back\-end services. **THE CANDIDATE** * 2\+ years of experience * Expertise with web client languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript) * Fluency with web client frameworks (React, Node), remote API design and architecture (REST, WebSockets) * Experience with source code management (Git is a plus) * Experience with databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, or KDB * Object oriented design skills and/or familiarity with Java a plus * Python experience is a plus * Interest in trading and financial market This role is based in our Dublin office with some flexibility to work hybrid. **THE PROCESS** Following an initial resume screen, candidates will be invited to complete an online programming test. Those who perform well will be contacted to arrange a phone screen. *Virtu Financial is an equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, welcoming you for who you are and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.*

title: Fund Accountant
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions and enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive at every stage of your career. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** ------------------- Alternative Investments Associates and Accountants work as part of a team responsible for providing accounting, administration and investor servicing for hedge funds, private equity funds and closed\-end registered products. Individuals calculate net asset value(s) and disseminate financial and performance information to the investment advisor and their investors. Acting as primary fund accountant on multiple relationships, ensures all fund accounting functions are in completed in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). Records trade information, income/expense activity and applies valuation of securities in the portfolio accounting system. Enters and reconciles all investor contributions and withdrawals in the partnership accounting system. Allocates fund level profits to individual investors for preparation and distribution of investor statements. Reviews and coordinates fund expense analysis and expense processing. Prepares fund financial statements, audit and tax schedules. Basic Qualifications * High school diploma or equivalent Preferred Skills/Experience * Basic knowledge of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) * Ability to identify and resolve/escalate complex problems with minimal guidance * Ability to manage multiple tasks/projects and deadlines simultaneously * Effective interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills * Proficient computer navigation skills using a variety of software packages including Microsoft Office applications **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * **Clearly defined salary ranges** aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * **Performance\-based incentives** for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * **Inclusive equitable benefits** that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * **Continuous development opportunities** including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * **Employee recognition programs** that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Fund Accounting(Alternatives) Associate 1
description: **Who We Are Looking For** YOU! A dynamic and enthusiastic individual who is looking to work in an exciting and evolving sector. As part of our current expansion of operations in Ireland, we have a number of roles at graduate and entry level available within our Alternatives Fund Accounting Group where you can enhance you career through experience gained in multiple facets including: * Hedge Funds * Crypto Funds * Hybrid Funds Join us if making your mark in the Alternatives Servicing Sector is a challenge you are up for. **What can we offer you?** We are the largest Fund Administrator in Ireland, servicing global market leading clients across a variety of product types. With us, you have the opportunity to develop your career in many different directions within the funds industry. This is a team providing a crucial operational service for clients worldwide, and you will work with a range of internal teams across the globe to service a wide array of prominent clients across multiple fund types. We offer flexibility to blend working from home or office, excellent benefits package, strong personal and career development programs to support your career journey and much more. **As a Fund Accounting Associate, you will be involved in:** * Preparing daily cash, position and market value reconciliations including trade capture and pricing. * Assist in the Dealing Date accounting process. * Work on complex financial instruments such as equities, fixed income, futures options and swaps. * Participate in client related or internally driven projects. * Prepare clients’ related correspondence (e.g. client requests, audit confirmations) * Assist Managers in semi\-annual and annual audit process. * Identify any unusual or potentially suspicious transaction activity and report and/or escalate in accordance with corporate policy and guidelines detailed in relevant operating procedures. **What We Value** These skills will help you succeed in this role * Strong analytical skills and an attention to detail and accuracy. * Strong problem\-solving skills. * Ability to perform effectively within strict client deadlines. * Ability to escalate where appropriate. **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * Degree level at any discipline is preferred, but not essential. **About State Street** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-790911

Pay: €14\.47 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Application question(s): * When are you available to start? * Are you available to work a set shift of Sunday\- Thursday, 6pm\-10pm? title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: LotusWorks is an Engineering Services provider specializing in managing Commissioning, Construction Services, Calibration, Operations \& Maintenance scopes of work within global manufacturing facilities. LotusWorks operates globally across EMEA and North America partnering with the world's leading manufacturers across the Semiconductor, Pharmaceutical \& Biologics, Medical Device and Data Centre sectors. Our team of Engineering and Technical professionals is immersed in cutting\-edge technologies and innovations. LotusWorks is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, integral to our people\-first approach. As a Graduate Electrical Engineer (Commissioning), you will support the Electrical Commissioning Team in the execution of electrical commissioning activities on Data Centre projects across Europe. This role combines hands\-on field commissioning with office\-based engineering support, providing an excellent opportunity to develop technical expertise in electrical systems, infrastructure, controls, and commissioning processes. Working alongside experienced engineers and technicians, you will receive structured training, mentoring, and field exposure to build competence in commissioning activities from Level 2 (Installation Verification) through to Level 5 (Integrated System Testing). This role is designed to support your progression from a graduate engineer to an independent commissioning professional. Travel to project sites across Europe for extended periods is a core requirement of this position. **Responsibilities** * Support electrical commissioning activities across Data Centre projects under the guidance of experienced commissioning personnel. * Participate in electrical design reviews and assist in identifying and documenting design discrepancies. * Review project specifications, control philosophies, System of Operation (SOO), Description of Operation (DOO), and equipment submittals. * Read and interpret electrical drawings, including single\-line diagrams, schematics, and layouts. * Assist in the development and review of commissioning test scripts and procedures. * Witness and support Level 2 to Level 5 commissioning activities, including Installation Verification Checks (IVC), Pre\-Functional Checks (PFC), Functional Performance Tests (FPT), and Integrated System Tests (IST). * Execute assigned commissioning activities once trained and authorised by the Electrical Lead. * Accurately record test results, field observations, and commissioning documentation. * Assist with preparation of commissioning reports, trackers, dashboards, and As\-Left documentation. * Attend project meetings and provide status updates on assigned activities. * Support the review of commissioning documentation and document management systems. * Any other duties or responsibilities that may be assigned to you in your role. **Skills Required** * Strong interest in electrical engineering and commissioning within mission\-critical environments. * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Willingness to learn and develop technical knowledge through training and practical experience. * Ability to travel extensively across Europe for project assignments. * Commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality. **Education / Licenses / Certifications** * Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related engineering discipline. * Recent graduates or candidates with relevant internship/work placement experience are encouraged to apply. * Full driving licence preferred. * Proof of education, licenses, and certifications will be required where applicable **Benefits** * VHI Healthcare Cover * Life Assurance * Pension Contribution * Training And Educational Assistance * Annual GP Check\-Up * Extra Annual Leave * Healthcare Screening * Recognition Rewards *LotusWorks is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered.*

title: Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Role Purpose** The role of a Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer is to execute engineering and design activities of the project. To conduct design reviews, project engineering, commissioning support ensuring consistent application of agreed procedures and utilization of standard forms. Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer’s Roles \& Responsibilities **General** * Assist and conduct the mechanical engineering and design activities on the project. * Support mechanical package owners. * Ensure the design management process is being followed on the project by either taking on the role directly. **EHS** * Read and understand EHS Plan. * Read, understand and promote the mechanical safe systems of work (MSSW) **Procurement** * Assist in the production of pre\-qualification packs (PQP) for design services and RFQ’s for equipment. Forward to project quantity surveyor for Subcontractor Package or Procurement Manager for Purchases. **RFQ Shall Include** * Scope of Works * Drawings and Specifications * Compliance, Deviation and Exclusion Matrices * Schedule \- deliveries, installation and commissioning requirements PQQ shall be in line with MP17 \- Selection and appointment of design consultants. * Mark\-up equipment and design services quotes to advise the project manager of the correct scope that aligns with the technical submittal and design brief. * Develop and update procurement \& design schedule/tracker with project planner. Continuously update the tracker until all technical submittals achieve status A, design deliverables accepted and materials/equipment have been delivered. **Quality** * Be engaged and utilize the documentation workflow for the project. Documentation generation, peer review, submission, re\-submission, distribution etc. * Review, comment and understand Quality Plan. * Assist in the development the project scope specific ITP considering project specification, client and local requirements. * Assist in the development of system tracker including equipment detail, interfacing details (electrical \& control), commissioning checklists and walkdown requirements * Review and understand any local authority requirements. * Develop lessons learned log of the project and continually update. * Indicate what the learning was * What the outcome or mitigations were * Indicate whether it was an internal finding or should be reported externally * Indicate if it was a cost, time or both impacts. * Assist in the O\&Ms production in line with the specification and agreed program. **Planning** * Understand and provide input and updates to the project schedule. * Input ROJ dates of CFCI equipment onto the equipment tracker. * Request and monitor the OFCI delivery dates. **Engineering \& Design** * Complete familiarisation and technical reviews on projects and record notes. Pose questions to the estimators and submit relevant RFI’s for clarifications on design/ scope. * Assist in the development of systems tracker along with equipment tag references, details and interfaces. * Assist in the development and tracking of design responsibility matrix/ contractor design portion (CDP). * Assist with the development and input into Design Deviation and Exclusions Log to ensure the team are aware of the agreed deviations and exclusions from design. * Validate the design using the request for information (RFI), design review process and performing the appropriate high level technical calculations. * Assist in the development of schematic’s when gaps exist in the design. * Review and understand The BIM execution plan, the Commissioning Plan and the Tracking Plan for the project. * Review the federated BIM model. * Review the coordinated drawings for construction to ensure it is in compliance with the P\&ID’s and schematics and that there is sufficient access to plant and equipment for commissioning, maintenance and replacement. * Development and track Technical Submittals until Status A. * Assist in the management of change from a technical perspective, updating change logs and schedules as required. * Tag Devices and instrumentation, spot each device on site in consultation with controls vendor and designer. * Attend biweekly technical meetings with members of the design team, subcontractors and internal project engineering team. * Assist in the verification of pipe and pump, duct and fan sizing and ensure design selections are suitable. * Assist in the production of the O\&M manuals in compliance with the specification. * Execute the project design and engineering process to ensure compliance. * Assist in technical review of tenders when required. * Attend and contribute to engineering and design kick\-off meeting at the outset of the project and record the minutes when required. * Attend and contribute to weekly engineering and design meetings and record the minutes when required. * Own action items identified during meetings and close them in a timely manner. **Communication** * Contribute to the development of weekly engineering and design reports and trackers. * Populate the project risk log and highlight any risks that arise to the project manager. * Manage and resolve risks and potential roadblocks with mitigation and management plans. **Commercial** * Understand the commercial fundamentals of a project including, budgets, material substation. * Understand commercial impacts of potential variation and assist the quantity surveyor in compiling variations. * Advise the project quantity surveyor and the project manager of any potential cost impacts to the project on an ongoing basis. **HR** * Attend CPD workshops and events. * Proactively and vigilantly keep information secure and fully comply with the Kirby Information Security Management System (ISMS).

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Greek Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Greek * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate/Junior EHS Officer
description: **Company Information and Introduction:** Weltec is seeking a motivated Graduate / Trainee Environmental, Health \& Safety (EHS) Officer to join our team and support a variety of construction and engineering projects in the Leixlip area. This is an excellent opportunity for an emerging EHS professional to gain hands‑on experience while working closely with the EHS Manager and collaborating with Site Project Managers across multiple sites. **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** * Work collaboratively with our Client to ensure full compliance with all relevant Health, Safety, and Environmental legislation and industry standards. * Prepare and review Method Statements, Risk Assessments, and other EHS documentation. * Conduct scheduled and unscheduled EHS audits, inspections, and incident investigations and assist in implementing corrective and preventive actions. * Provide ongoing advice to Project Management teams on EHS best practices, ensuring a proactive safety culture across all sites. * Plan, prepare, and deliver EHS\-related training, including Toolbox Talks and site inductions as required. * Monitor, analyse, and report on EHS performance metrics, highlighting trends and improvement opportunities. * Support the EHS Department with administrative duties, document control, and continuous improvement initiatives. * Assist in maintaining and developing the company’s EHS Management System, ensuring policies and procedures are up to date and effectively implemented. **Key Skills and Competencies:*** Completed or currently be undertaking a recognised qualification in Health \& Safety, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Experience in the construction sector; however, this is not essential as full training will be provided. * Experience working within pharmaceutical, medical technology, or other highly regulated environments is desirable but not required. * Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build positive working relationships with site teams, clients, and external stakeholders. * Be proactive, adaptable, and committed to promoting a safe working environment and a customer\-focused approach. * Possess strong IT skills, particularly within Microsoft Word, Excel, and general reporting tools. * Show a willingness to learn, develop professionally, and contribute to continuous improvement efforts within the EHS function. Weltec offers a competitive salary, ongoing professional development, and a supportive environment where you can grow your career in EHS. The successful candidate will gain exposure to a wide range of projects, systems, and compliance frameworks, making this an ideal role for someone looking to establish a strong foundation in the EHS profession. *Please note, we do not accept CVs or candidate profiles from recruitment agencies where Weltec terms of business have not been signed. Additionally, we will not consider or agree to payment of any recruiter fee under these circumstances. This also applies to CVs or candidate profiles sent directly to any Weltec Hiring Managers.*

title: HVAC Technician
description: **Who We Are** As a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings, our mission is to reimagine the performance of buildings to serve people, places, and the planet. Join a winning team that enables you to build your best future! Our teams are uniquely positioned to support a multitude of industries across the globe. You will have the opportunity to develop yourself through meaningful work projects and learning opportunities. We strive to provide our employees with an experience, focused on supporting their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. We are a leading provider of equipment, controls and services for heating, ventilating, air\-conditioning \& refrigeration systems! The UK\&I Service team delivers innovative maintenance and service solutions to a wide customer base across all industry segments for HVAC, chillers, controls, and mechanical \& electrical works. We offer the largest portfolio of HVAC equipment and controls in the world, redefining the environments where people live, work, learn and play! We are the power behind our customers’ missions! We are looking for a Dublin / Leinster based, HVAC Technician to join our growing team of specialised YORK Chiller technicians commissioning and maintaining YORK chiller equipment and supporting customer’s facilities. Become a member of the Johnson Controls family and thrive in an empowering company culture where your voice and ideas will be heard – your next great opportunity is just a few clicks away! **For the right candidate we offer** * Competitive salary, * Paid Overtime and travel time * On\-Call Allowance. * Lunch Allowance. * Paid Overnight expenses where applicable * Performance based annual pay review * Productivity Bonus Incentives * New Business Identification incentive scheme * Employee Referral program * Paid Annual Leave \- 25 days plus bank holidays * Holiday buy back option available. * Comprehensive benefits package \- Pension, Health Insurance \& Life Assurance. * Company Vehicle, Phone, Laptop \& IPAD and specialist test Equipment provided. * 39 Hour week\- Monday\- Thursday (8am\- 4:30pm) \& Friday (8am\-3:30pm). * YORK refrigeration and air conditioning Products Factory Training * Encouraging, Career developmental \& collaborative team environment * Dedication to safety through our Zero Harm policy **What You Will Do** You will provide up to date site surveys, records, and reports regarding works completed and actions needed to aid quoting of new or replacement equipment. Your main role is to carry Commissioning, servicing, trouble shooting, and maintenance of Industrial water/air cooled Chillers and Heat pumps. As a Chiller Service Engineer, you are the primary contact for all technical service issues, accountable for resolving highly complex systems within a set timeframe and according to a defined process, service agreement and customer needs. You will be working mainly with the company’s largest customers in the Leinster area, predominantly within the foreign direct investment sector of Pharmaceutical, Life science and High Tech Clients. Health and Safety will be at the forefront of everything you do. Although often working without accompaniment you will be part of a team and contribute towards its success. **What We Look For** * You will have a Refrigeration F\-GAS 2079 Certificate. * Ammonia Experience would be a distinct advantage. * Relevant Trade Qualification in Refrigeration, or A qualified industrial electrician willing to cross train on the York Chiller and heat pumps. * Safe Pass Card \& Manual Handling Certificate * Qualified to a minimum of NFQ 5 * Familiar and capable of delivering Service/Maintenance and repair of Water\-cooled and Air\-cooled Chillers * Commissioning Experience on large ton Centrifugal and Screw chillers and heat pumps would be a distinct advantage but factory training will be provided. * Have experience with major Chiller manufacturers such as York/ Sabroe/ Trane/ Carrier/ Daikin. * Capable of working without detailed procedures and with limited supervision. * Full Clean Irish Driving License

title: HVAC Utilities Engineer
description: **Location** Dublin, Leinster, Ireland **Job ID** R\-239533 **Date posted** 02/06/2026 **This is what you will do:** The HVAC Utilities Engineer is responsible for ensuring that all site HVAC utilities systems and controlled Temperature Units perform predictably and continue to operate as required in order to support the manufacturing operations process. The HVAC Utilities Engineer also ensures that all utility systems conform to relevant regulatory requirements as well as company specifications and requirements. title: Health and Safety Graduate
description: We are looking for a Graduate Health and Safety Officers to join our Health and Safety Team. This is an 18\-month fixed term, site\-based graduate position. **Key responsibilities:** Typically our Health \& Safety Graduates will be expected to undertake and assist with the following duties: * Work as part of the Cairn Homes Construction management team to ensure the provision of a safe and productive work environment. * Always seek to improve the standard of Health \& Safety in a practical manner within the company management system and on specific sites. * Monitor and enforce the implementation of Cairn Homes Health \& Safety Management System. * Identify site specific hazards and ensure they are addressed in the site documentation and in a practical manner on the site. * Ensure clear lines of communication are maintained to the H\&S Manager. * Ensure the site folders and statutory records are being maintained on site. * Ensure strict adherence to site rules by all contractors. * Assist site management to comply with statutory H\&S requirements. * Assist Health \& Safety Manager to develop and implement Risk assessments and improvements to the Cairn Homes Safe Systems of Work. * Assist Site management to ensure Contractor Compliance on Cairn Homes Sites. * Assist H\&S Manager and Site management to coordinate the safe systems of work for contractors. * Enforce site specific disciplinary procedures. * Carry out H\&S auditing on site and address non\-conformances as they are identified with site management and contractors. * Contribute to accident and incident investigation. * Contribute to the Tool Box talk topics, discussion and delivery. **The candidate:** * 3rd Level Qualification in Health \& safety * Relevant work experience desirable * A willingness to attend training and to develop relevant knowledge, techniques and skills. * To have strong analytical skills and ability to present findings * To have good spoken and written communication skills * To be methodical, pay attention to detail and be accurate * To be good at working with people at all levels and willing to work in a team. * To be self\-motivated with ability to work on own initiative * To be able to co\-ordinate a number of different projects at the same time. * To be willing to accept responsibility for completing designated tasks. **Working with Cairn Homes:** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join Cairn which include; * Competitive salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Full health insurance with Irish Life health * Annual salary review * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Free Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * Employee Assistance Programme with VHI * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture Committee * Talent Development Programmes * Approved Profit Sharing Scheme (APSS) The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good

description: **Launch Your International Career with Purpose and Adventure** Are you searching for a **rewarding, entry\-level job** that lets you **travel the world** , **work abroad** , and gain **real\-world experience** all while making a difference? title: Integration Engineer (Client facing) Professional Services
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Stripe’s Professional Services team works with our most strategic users. You will work on the highest visibility projects to provide technical expertise to our customers to help accelerate their knowledge, solution design and integrations with Stripe. This role is responsible for leading strategic conversations and developing plans that will transform the payments and financial infrastructure for leading enterprises. What you’ll do Responsibilities \* Plan and execute integration engineering end\-to\-end, from high\-level architecture down to code implementation. This includes conducting discovery sessions with users to understand their goals, parsing out specific use cases, and guiding users to implement high complexity integrations. * Think on your feet and communicate technical concepts, explaining how Stripe works from both a product feature and API architecture perspective. Manage user expectations on timelines and product changes. * Be the primary, ongoing technical point of contact for our largest users' and partners' technical teams. You will be responsible for resolving user integration issues and owning escalations for operational issues and priority requests. * Collaborate with Sales, Solution Architecture and Implementation teams to drive client engagement, customer wins, and revenue on behalf of the Professional Services team * Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering to proactively identify potential enhancements and new features based on feedback you’ll gather from the field. * Provide deep technical support and debugging, inspecting and understanding the Stripe codebase and thoroughly testing functionality to best advise users, while also building and demonstrating proof\-of\-concept integrations, creating user\-specific test plans, and answering ad\-hoc technical questions. * In some cases, lead "Hands On Keyboard" implementations for user engagements, providing material feedback on coding practices, CI/CD, and software architecture. * Review and optimize existing user integrations and provide recommendations to improve performance, reliability, and efficiency. * Build tools and analytics; create dashboards, run queries, and generate custom data reports to support user business reviews and operational needs. * Develop and share knowledge, building reusable processes to enable the larger Professional Services team. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements \* 4 years \+ experience in a Software Engineering / Integration Engineering role. * Ability to write code (in programming languages like Go, Java, C/C\+\+ etc.) and quickly debug user code snippets. * Comfortable with various code\-level debugging (Stripe code and user code) and using code as a tool for problem\-solving. * Strong generalist technical experience, including proficiency in SQL and an understanding of data structures and algorithms. * Understanding of the payments domain and API security topics. * Be comfortable talking about technical concepts, and explaining how Stripe works, both from a product features and API architecture perspective. * A user first approach \& empathy with our users, capable of mapping business requirements into reliable technical solutions. * Confident and comfortable with customers, with the ability to lead strategic technical conversations. Preferred requirements \* Enjoy the puzzle of solving open\-ended problems, both individually and as a member of our team. Willingness to learn and teach others, including mentoring newer team members. * Collaborative, communicative, consultative; able to apply specific methodologies to ensure repeatability of engagements. * Intellectually curious, with great problem solving skills. * Technical writing skills to document complex integration guidance and processes. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €104,000 \- €156,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.
