This role is ideal for a **Junior CSV Engineer** seeking hands‑on experience with system governance, compliance, and GxP operations. You will play a key role in ensuring that all systems are accessed securely, appropriately, and in line with regulatory requirements. This is a great opportunity to develop within a highly regulated, fast‑paced environment with strong career progression potential **Key Responsibilities** * Manage user access requests across all onsite systems, ensuring correct permissions and adherence to internal procedures. * Maintain and update access control documentation, including user access matrices and audit trails. * Support system owners with account creation, modification, and deactivation. * Ensure compliance with GxP, data integrity, and CSV (Computer System Validation) requirements. * Assist in periodic access reviews and internal/external audit preparation. * Troubleshoot basic IT access issues and escalate more complex problems to the relevant teams. * Collaborate with IT, Quality, and CSV teams to ensure systems remain compliant and secure. * Contribute to continuous improvement of access management processes and SOPs **.** **Skills \& Experience Required** * Experience **in IT Administration, Service Desk, System Support, or CSV** within a regulated or technical environment. * Understanding **of user access management, permissions, and system security principles.** * Experience in the pharmaceutical, biotech, or medical device sector a strong advantage * Exposure to systems such as LIMS, MES, ERP, QMS, or other GxP‑regulated platforms beneficial * Familiarity with **GxP, data integrity, or CSV** concepts * Strong attention to detail and ability to follow structured procedures. * Excellent communication skills and ability to work with cross‑functional teams. * Proactive approach to problem‑solving and process improvement.

title: Junior Consultant – Full training provided
description: * **Expert training from day one \- start strong, learn fast, and crush it from the jump.** * **Constant growth \- never settle, always level up, and keep your skills unstoppable.** * **Certified \& credible \- earn your stripes, stand out, and own your field.** Are you stuck in customer service or sales, hitting targets but seeing no real growth? Ready for a career where your results actually fuel your success. At **Access Healthcare** , we specialize in helping driven professionals from all kinds of backgrounds launch successful careers in recruitment. Our team is growing, and we’re looking for ambitious people ready to thrive in a fast\-paced, results\-driven environment and become Recruitment Consultants with us. **Why choose Access Healthcare for your career move?** * **From Zero to Expert:** 200\+ training modules, 1:1 coaching, and continuous development, you’ll never feel stuck. * **Continuous Development:** You’ll never feel stagnant. With nonstop training, you’ll keep developing your skills and growing your career. * **Get Certified:** Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant and build credibility that opens doors. **What You Will Need** * At least 2 year of experience in a sales\-focused role (e.g., Sales Rep, Sales Associate, Account Executive, Business Development, Inside sales, Tele sales representative, Sales Manager, Customer Success Manager, etc.) * An ambitious, growth\-driven mindset * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent is a plus but not required. **Total Compensation (Average Performer)** * Year 1: **€45,000\+** * Year 2: **€75,000\+** * Base salary: DOE * Commission: Uncapped, unlimited earning potential * Top performers earn significantly above these figures Ready for a new, exciting opportunity? Contact Maria de Gouveia at maria.degouveia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1202 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity. Take your career to new heights!

title: Junior JavaScript Developer
description: Arabian Private Holdings is looking for a Junior JavaScript Developer to contribute to the development of our internal proprietary tools. This role is focused on building functional interfaces that translate complex corporate data into readable formats for our firm principals. We are currently collaborating with a research and software group in Ireland to build out a new data\-visualization layer for our portfolio holding companies. This specific activity requires technical support that can coordinate with these partners during their business hours while working as part of our remote distributed team. **Employment Setup** This is a fully remote role where the legal employer is Arabian Private Holdings, based in Saudi Arabia. This position is open to candidates based in Ireland who already possess the legal right to work in their country of residence, as we do not provide visa sponsorship or local employment contracts. The arrangement is a full\-time, permanent engagement with attractive pay provided. You will report directly to our Lead Technical Architect. Our hiring process involves an initial review of your technical background, a small JavaScript coding exercise, and a final conversation with a firm principal. We require at least four hours of overlap with the GMT\+3 timezone to facilitate team coordination and peer reviews. **About Arabian Private Holdings** Our firm was founded on the principle that many critical business problems do not require a massive consulting team or a hundred\-page deck. Instead, they require a direct and thorough view of the situation. We advise family groups, listed corporations, and government entities on corporate strategy and operating model performance. Because we focus on simplicity over complexity, our digital tools must be equally direct. We operate as a remote firm, choosing to prioritize the quality of our analysis and the strength of our relationships over traditional office space. This structure allows us to focus entirely on the mandates we accept, ensuring that every piece of software we build serves a specific, logical purpose for our clients. **What You Will Do** * Develop and maintain UI components using modern JavaScript to support our portfolio dashboards. * Translate logic from strategy models into interactive web\-based visualizations. * Work closely with the Lead Architect to maintain a clean and lightweight codebase. * Debug frontend performance issues to ensure our reporting tools remain responsive. * Write straightforward documentation for the features and components you build. * Collaborate with the strategy team to refine how complex ownership data is displayed. * Implement responsive designs that allow principals to access data on various devices. * Participate in peer code reviews to ensure consistency across our internal tools and systems. **What We Are Looking For** * Basic familiarity with JavaScript (ES6\+) and an understanding of core programming logic. * Some exposure to modern frontend libraries such as React, Vue, or Svelte. * Ability to write clean HTML and CSS to create functional, distraction\-free interfaces. * A basic understanding of version control systems like Git for code management. * Attention to detail when following design specifications or data requirements. * Comfort working in a remote environment that relies heavily on written communication. * An interest in learning how technical tools can support high\-level corporate decision\-making. **What This Role Offers** * Direct mentorship from a Lead Technical Architect with deep industry knowledge. * The opportunity to build professional tools used by senior leaders in major organizations. * A working environment that values deep focus and logical precision over meetings. * A fully remote setup that allows for a stable and consistent professional routine. * Exposure to the intersection of frontend development and high\-level corporate strategy. * Professional growth within a firm that prioritizes directness and technical quality.

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: We're looking for a curious, driven professional who's passionate about marketing and wants to grow its strategic experience. If you're someone who spots a great shot, has a strong eye for design, and is curious to learn and ambitious to grow we are looking for you. This isn't a desk\-only role. You'll be out on\-site, capturing the work we do, and then turning that into compelling content across our channels. The role sits at the heart of the business, reporting directly to the Sales Manager and working closely with, fabrication, purchasing, and external partners. **Who We Are** Celuplast is a fast\-growing specialist supplier and fabricator of building products, serving trade markets across Ireland. We operate across trade counter, online sales, fabrication systems, and specialist product categories including landscaping, interiors, roofing, and home extension solutions. We're part of a wider group with four companies across Ireland and the UK, all focused on building products. That means there's a broader network to collaborate with, share ideas across, and for the right person, plenty of room to grow. **What You'll Be Doing** **Day\-to\-Day** • Create and manage content across our social media channels, keeping things consistent, on\-brand, and engaging • Get out on\-site to photograph and film our products, projects, and people — strong photography skills are a must • Updating the Marketing Calendar • Edit video content for use across social, web, and other channels • Coordinate with website developer so the message is consistent across product listings and landing pages. • Plan and send email marketing campaigns (at least twice a month) to trade and homeowner audiences • Support product launches with well\-timed, well\-designed assets across all channels • Collaborate with our PIM system to keep product information accurate and up to date • Create marketing collateral including brochures, flyers, presentations and digital assets. • Learn and contribute to campaign planning and execution. **What We're Looking For** **Essential** • 3\+ years of experience in design and content creation. • Strong photography skills — comfortable shooting on\-site in a real\-world, non\-studio environment • Video editing skills • Social media management experience • Experience with email marketing tools and campaigns • A genuine eye for design this is a big asset in how we evaluate candidates • Proactivity and curiosity. • Excellent Communication Skills **Nice to Have** • Google Analytics Experience • Experience working with KPIs and reporting on campaign performance • Familiarity with PIM systems or product data management • Some exposure to paid media or basic campaign strategy • Experience in a trade, construction, or building products environment • Experience in e\-commerce. **What Success Looks Like** We're not just looking for activity, we want progress. Here's how we'll measure it together: • We’ll create a plan to grow our social media following (targeting new followers per month) • Maintaining a steady organic engagement across channels • Generating leads through social media and website activity • Running email campaigns twice a month, on time and on brief • Delivering monthly reports that show ROI and conversion metrics, and knowing what to do with the data **Why Join Us?** You'll be joining a business that's growing, has a clear direction, and genuinely wants its marketing to match its ambitions. This is a role where you can shape things, not just maintain them, and build a track record that could take you into a senior position within the group. Beyond the work itself, we're a team that looks after each other. You'll find good craic in the day\-to\-day, regular social events, and a culture that takes giving back seriously as we actively support non\-profit causes as part of who we are. You'll also get real exposure to the wider building and construction community across Ireland and the UK, expanding your professional network in an industry that rewards relationships. If you're ready to get your hands dirty, pick up a camera, and make a real difference to how a growing Irish business presents itself to the world, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Junior Marketing Executive (Graduate Role)
description: **About Unify Brands** *Unify brands* is a leading sales and marketing company, which brings together a unique portfolio of exciting consumer brands. We specialise in growing brands and delivering success for our partners. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated and creative Junior Marketing Executive to join our team on an initial 3\-month fixed\-term contract, with the possibility of extending the role based on performance and business needs. This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate who wants hands\-on experience across a wide range of marketing activities, from social media and email marketing to influencer campaigns and live brand activations. Working with brands like Perfect Ted, DASH water, Candy Kittens and many more. Reporting to the Marketing Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the day\-to\-day marketing efforts for both Unify Brands and the portfolio of brands we represent. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage and schedule social media content across platforms, including Instagram \& LinkedIn. * Create engaging content such as graphics, videos, and captions * Update and maintain e\-commerce websites, including product listings and promotional banners * Build and send email marketing campaigns * Work on Meta Ads with the external ads manager that runs our account * Assist with influencer outreach, gifting, and campaign coordination * Support the planning and execution of brand activations, sampling events, and pop\-ups * Assist with general marketing administration and coordination **About You** * Recent graduate in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field * Strong interest in consumer brands, digital marketing, and social media * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Highly organised with strong attention to detail * Proactive and eager to learn in a fast\-paced environment * Comfortable managing multiple projects at once * Creative mindset and enthusiasm for new ideas **Desirable Skills** * Experience with Canva, CapCut, or Adobe Creative Suite * Familiarity with email marketing platforms such as Klaviyo * Experience using Shopify or other e\-commerce platforms – not required but a plus * Understanding of influencer marketing and social media trends * Basic knowledge of analytics and reporting tools * Experience using AI tools such as Chat GPT **Why Join Us?** * Gain hands\-on experience across all areas of marketing * Work with exciting challenger brands * Opportunity to contribute directly to campaigns and activations * Potential for the role to be extended beyond the initial contract * Join a small, ambitious team where your work will have real impact Application question(s): * Are you a marketing graduate? Education: * Bachelor's (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor \- Seven Mills** Cairn has a unique and exciting opportunity for a high calibre Junior Quantity Surveyor to work with and to assist in the provision of quantity surveying services for post\-contract duties on a fast moving large residential development under construction. This role reports to the Senior Quantity Surveyor. This role is based on our Seven Mills site. The successful person will be an experienced, motivated team player who thrives in a fast\-paced dynamic environment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Typically, the candidate will be expected to undertake and assist with the following duties: * Assisting the Senior QS to manage subcontract packages and overseeing payments to subcontractors, suppliers and consultants along with normal QS duties. * Experience of RC frame / apartment and residential building / commercial projects ideal, but not essential * Assisting the Senior QS to assist with the preparation of monthly internal valuations and monthly cost reports * Reporting on budgets and variations. * Maintaining subcontractor and supplier contractual details * Tasks will be allocated to reflect increasing levels of development and experience. **The Candidate:** * Post\-Graduate experience with a PQS or Contractor. * An ability to achieve demanding time and quality targets. * Good IT skills with competency using Cubit, Cost\-ex or equivalent, Microsoft Word and Excel * A willingness to attend training and to develop relevant knowledge, techniques, and skills. * To have strong analytical skills and ability to present findings * To have good spoken and written communication skills * To be methodical, pay attention to detail and be accurate * To be good at working with people at all levels and willing to work in a team. * To be self\-motivated with ability to work on own initiative * To be able to co\-ordinate a number of different projects at the same time. * To be willing to accept responsibility for completing designated tasks. **Working with Cairn Homes:** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. When you join Cairn, your suite of benefits include: * A competitively benchmarked salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme * Family Health Insurance with Irish Life Health * Paid maternity and paternity leave * Annual leave days * Additional ‘free’ Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * VHI employee assistance programme for you and your family * SpectrumLife health and wellbeing programme * Workplace wellbeing events * Volunteer days * Provision of all site safety equipment provided (if required) * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Employee share plan * Culture Committee * Continuous professional development (CPD) programme and funding * Talent development programmes The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.

title: Junior Recruitment Administrator - CPM Ireland
description: **\*\*\* 3 Month Fixed Term Contract \*\*\*** Every member of the CPM team contributes to the overall growth of our business. People are at the core of CPM’s proposition, and as a member of the Human Resources team, this role will support the delivery of a positive employee experience and contribute to a culture of continuous improvement. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at an early stage in their career to gain hands\-on experience in recruitment and HR administration within a fast\-paced environment. A positive attitude, strong attention to detail, and a willingness to learn are key to success in this role. **Role Overview** The primary focus of this role is to provide administrative and coordination support to the Recruitment function within the business. You will assist with day\-to\-day recruitment activities while ensuring adherence to internal policies and company values: **We Care, We’re Curious, and We Achieve Together**. **Key Responsibilities** * Support hiring managers and the HR team with recruitment administration * Review job adverts and documentation for accuracy, spelling, and formatting * Post job vacancies across job boards and other platforms as directed * Schedule interviews and prepare interview packs * Coordinate with interview panels to organise interview logistics * Assist with onboarding administration, including issuing contracts and induction packs * Track completion of new starter documentation and follow up where required * Maintain accurate records on recruitment systems * Provide general administrative support to the HR team and assist with ad hoc projects **Skills \& Experience** **Essential:** * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Good organisational skills and attention to detail * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint) * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines * Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work with different stakeholders * Proactive attitude and willingness to learn * A digital mindset, with an interest in using technology to improve efficiency **Desirable:** * Previous administrative or customer service experience * Exposure to recruitment, HR, or office environments (e.g., internship, placement, or part\-time role) **Measures of Success** * Accurate and timely completion of recruitment administration tasks * Positive feedback from hiring managers and candidates * High level of data accuracy within recruitment systems * Meeting internal timelines and service level expectations **Contract Details** * **Salary:** €26,000 (pro\-rated for contract duration) * **Working Hours:** 35 hours per week **Benefits** * Flexible working environment * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and development opportunities * Exposure to a dynamic HR and recruitment function

title: Junior Systems Analyst
description: Canada Life Europe (CLE) employs over 600 people across its Irish and German offices and serves over 500,000 customers in the German market. It focuses on pensions and individual protection products, offering a combination of unit\-linked funds, unitised\-with\-profits funds and variable annuity business within its pension products. Currently CLE is amongst the top six companies in the German broker market and its goal is to become a Top 3 Broker Provider in the market. CLE is seeking a Junior Systems Analyst to join our System Analysts’ team within Information Services (I.S.). The overall I.S. function has over 120 people in the team. **Role Overview** Canada Life Europe requires a Junior Systems Analyst to work on I.S. projects, initially on the Point of Sales Delivery team. This individual will work with the I.S. and Business teams based in Germany and Ireland, and the Canada Life Partners and Brokers based in Germany. The successful candidate will be assigned to work under the direction of the Lead Analyst. As a team member he/she will be expected to work effectively with other team members such as Systems and QA Analysts, Project Managers and Software Developers. This role is part of the I.S. Systems Analysis Team which is predominantly based in Dublin, Ireland. The successful candidate will be expected to work on programmes/projects across Germany and Ireland. **What you will help us to achieve** *The main duties and responsibilities included in this role:* * Become familiar with CLEs Point of Sales Systems and propose/implement changes as required to the systems. * Create and update technical specification documents for internal \& external use * Elicit, understand and clarify the requirements of the business stakeholders * Translate requirements into business and functional specifications * Work collaboratively with the Lead Analyst based in Ireland * Work with Systems Architects/Analysts to determine potential solutions and test them for both technical and business compatibility **What you will need to be successful in the role** *Essential skills and experience:* * 3rd level education preferably in a technical discipline, or equivalent technical experience * Knowledge of XML and XSD * Knowledge of data modelling and data formats * Ability to test and troubleshoot systems software * Understanding of recognised application/service delivery processes and methodology * Excellent communication skills * Self\-motivated with a positive and flexible attitude * Excellent team player * Ability to set and work to strict project deadlines *Desired skills and experience:* * Experience working as a Technical Analyst, ideally within the insurance industry, or a large multinational environment * Knowledge/experience of Confluence and Jira * Understanding of detailed analysis techniques with the ability to translate into comprehensive documentation which can be understood by Business stakeholders and I.S. alike. * Experience in electronic data exchange * Experience in at least one programming languages with the ability to write pseudo code * Experience working on eBusiness, Web Application and Web Services projects * Understanding of insurance business practices and processes **The Person** *The successful candidate will ideally:* * Be educated to technical degree level, or equivalent experience * Fluent in German and English * Have excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and documentation skills * Have some experience working as Technical Analyst * Have experience with XML, XSD, PDF Editors, MS Office Suite * Be a dynamic individual who is a motivated self\-starter and excellent team player * Have professional communication skills with a ‘can do’ attitude * Be customer focused and result driven The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Canada Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Canada Life by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Even if you don’t see yourself reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we encourage you to reach out and apply if you feel you are a good fit for the role. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive and diverse company, and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation and life experiences to apply. Canada Life Group Services is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact **lifecareers@irishlife.ie** and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. *CLGS supports Equal Opportunity and is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.*

title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Here’s what we value most: * Love for Food – You care about flavour, freshness, and making food that looks as good as it tastes. * Knife Skills – You know your way around a chopping board (or you’re eager to learn). Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Life Insurance Apprentice
description: **Who We Are** At Cornmarket, we look after the financial wellbeing of public servants and their families. We’ve been focused on public sector employees for over 50 years, learning how to bring our clients the best financial services – from car, home, health and life insurance, to income protection, retirement planning and beyond. **Role Purpose** We are looking for an Insurance Apprentice to join our Life Division. This is an initial three\-year fixed term earn learn contract, which upon successful completion, you will be offered a permanent opportunity within the business. While on the programme, in addition to gaining on\-the\-job experience, you will complete the finance industry APA and QFA professional qualifications as well as a Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Insurance Practice (Apprenticeship) which is Level 8 degree awarded by IT Sligo. You will receive a tailored induction plan and will be assigned a business supervisor/ mentor to provide support throughout the scheme. We will work with you to enable you to reach your development goals and aspirations for your career at Cornmarket. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is looking to carve out a career in financial services. **Accountabilities Of The Role** * Making outbound calls and taking calls from members of the public sector unions to arrange appointments with the company’s Consultants * Meeting monthly targets which are built around call volume and appointment scheduling (alongside these targets comes an excellent monthly bonus structure) * Dealing with clients in a professional and timely manner with a customer first approach * Cross\-selling other Cornmarket products and services * Performing tasks in a compliant manner to set procedures and prescribed scripts * Completing various administration duties, such as data entry, logging and scanning * Participating in weekly team meetings * Continuously looking for and suggesting ways to improve existing processes * Process’s new business applications using our Proposal tracking System * Ensure new business applications are compliant with defined checking questions * Provide support to the team that Issue life new business in accordance with the terms given by the underwriter * Process incoming correspondence and documentation effectively and efficiently * Work to meet and exceed targets for both call volume and processing of new business. * Provide support to the team that is responsible for dealing with client queries/requests in a professional and timely manner * Follow set procedures guidelines, identifying when it is necessary to escalate cases for alternative handling or manager’s advice * Communicate with Life offices, customers financial consultants regarding the progress of applications * Comply with risk control and regulatory requirements that are relevant to this role * Maintain commercial awareness relevant to this role **Entry Requirements** * Leaving Certificate Requirements: **Prior To 2017** * A minimum of 4 grade Ds at Ordinary Level and 2 Grade C3 at Higher Level * Leaving Certificate subjects must include Mathematics and English or Irish * An E grade in Higher Level Mathematics, and a B2 in Foundation Mathematics is a minimum entry requirement * A minimum of 140 points is required for entry to all Level 8 courses. **2017 Onwards** * A minimum of 4 grades 4o6/H7 and 2 grade H5 or higher Leaving Certificate subjects * Leaving Certificate subjects must include Mathematics and English or Irish * With English or Irish with O6/H7 and Mathematics with F2/o6/H7 * A minimum of 140 points is required for entry to all Level 8 courses * For students who are over 23 on January 1st of the year of entry, interviews and other selection tools will be used to assess suitability, replacing the minimum requirements above. * Learners who have a full FETAC level 5 qualification will also be eligible to join the programme. **What We Can Offer You?** * Flexible working from home options as part of our Cornmarket Flex ways of working * Significant investments in your professional development (e.g. insurance qualifications, professional qualifications relevant to your role) * Annual leave (increases based on service) * Flexi leave (option to take additional We’ve been focused on public sector employees for over 50 years, learning how to bring our clients the best financial services – from car, home, health and life insurance, to income protection, retirement planning and beyond. **Role Purpose** We are looking for an Insurance Apprentice to join our Life Division. title: ML Engineer
description: Job Description : Machine Learning Engineer ****About the Organization**** \- A global quantitative trading firm that combines deep research, cutting\-edge technology, and a collaborative culture. \- We build most of our systems from the ground up, and innovation is at the core of everything we do. \- As a Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll play a critical role in shaping the future of AI at the firm—enabling research at scale, accelerating experimentation, and unlocking opportunities across the organization. ****The Role**** \- A versatile ML Engineer to help build and optimize the core infrastructure that supports AI research. \- Works across the stack—from data processing and training efficiency to low\-level GPU programming and performance tooling. \- Contributes to reliable, scalable, high\-performance systems while enabling researchers to understand and improve their models. \- Ideal for someone comfortable navigating complex systems, collaborating across domains, and bringing a strong engineering mindset. ****What You’ll Do**** \- Design and implement high\-performance data pipelines for processing large\-scale datasets with emphasis on reliability and reproducibility \- Apply state\-of\-the\-art techniques to internal training runs to maximize hardware efficiency \- Implement custom GPU kernels \- Create observability and benchmarking tools to help researchers understand model and training performance \- Build and maintain secure sandboxed execution environments ****What We’re Looking For**** \- Experience with data pipeline development and ETL processes \- Strong systems programming skills and performance optimization \- Strong software engineering skills in Python \- Experience writing and optimizing custom GPU kernels \- Experience with observability, benchmarking, or performance\-focused infrastructure at scale \- Familiarity with AI/ML workloads \- Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast\-evolving environments and collaborating across disciplines ****Why Join Us**** \- Join a global quantitative trading firm that combines deep research, cutting\-edge technology, and a collaborative culture. \- We build most of our systems from the ground up, and innovation is at the core of everything we do. \- As a Machine Learning Engineer, you’ll play a critical role in shaping the future of AI at the firm—enabling research at scale, accelerating experimentation, and unlocking opportunities across the organization.

title: MV Specialist Electrical Engineer
description: **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is an international multi\-disciplinary engineering services contractor and leading provider of high\-value engineering services to a number of ‘blue chip’ companies. Operating across Ireland, the UK and Europe, Kirby has become the engineering service provider of choice in growth segments such as pharmaceuticals, data centres and power transmission and distribution. Our culture is innovative, collaborative and performance focused. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to grow and develop in a company committed to developing talent and rewarding performance. **Role Purpose** As a MV Specialist Electrical Engineer you will be responsible for the MV system related engineering activities on specific construction project or multiple projects. The activities associated with the project will vary and will be agreed with the Project Manager on an ongoing basis. You will co\-ordinate the technical design with the onsite installation through to the successful commissioning ensuring that the Kirby Group vision, mission \& values are fulfilled. Here's a detailed breakdown of the responsibilities: **Primary Responsibilities** * MV System Project Lead: Provide direction and leadership for the design and engineering MV system activities on a project level. This includes setting goals, developing short to mid\-term plans, and aligning the MV system progress with the overall project delivery strategy and program. * Multi\-Project Management: Lead and manage MV system design and engineering activities across various projects and geographical regions, depending on the scope and particular project requirements. * Compile and issue electrical technical submittals * Manage submittals through the approvals process, track procurement and delivery of electrical equipment and complete the receipt verification process once the equipment is delivered * Attend \& record regular supply chain meetings to ensure that progress is monitored \& maintained within the overall construction programme * Update and record progress on system trackers which are fully aligned with the construction schedule * Review the electrical installation with the BIM team to ensure the design is constructible and that there is adequate space for installation and maintenance of all equipment and cabling * Liaise with the design team \& supply chain to ensure correct operation of the plant on site \& successful handover * Complete electrical system walk\-downs to ensure systems are compliant with specifications and drawings * Compile test pack, FTOP’s and O\&Ms and present for approval * Manage specialist vendors during the commissioning process to ensure timely completion of their works * Undertake client training in line with agreed training plan * Regional Support: Assist construction teams with resolving complex MV system technical issues that cannot be resolved at the project level. **System Modelling \& Analysis** * Develop, review, and validate MV power system models using ETAP, PSCAD, DIgSILENT, CDEGS, or equivalent tools * Conduct advanced power system studies including: * Load flow analysis * Short\-circuit analysis (IEC/IEEE standards) * Protection coordination and selectivity studies * Motor starting analysis * Transient stability studies * Harmonic analysis \& power quality assessment * Arc\-flash hazard assessment * Grounding/resistivity studies (using CDEGS) * Ensure modelling assumptions, data inputs, and results are consistent with design documents and manufacturer data. **Design Review \& Validation** * Review consultant and subcontractor designs for MV systems (switchgear, transformers, cables, earthing, relays, and protection systems) to identify design gaps and deviations or contradictions in the specifications * Validate sizing, ratings, and specifications of MV equipment against system study outputs * Validate cable sizing and make recommendations as appropriate * Ensure designs comply with project specifications, IEC/IEEE standards, and client requirements * Verify integration of MV systems with LV, HV, and control systems **Leadership and Strategy** * Support the Project Manager, Electrical Engineering Manager and Design Manager to ensure the overall successful delivery of design and engineering MV system related activities on the project. * Attend monthly meetings with the Design Manager to ensure ongoing improvements and developments and to review KPI progress. * Talent Development: Oversee the onboarding and development of design and engineering team talent across the company. This includes supporting and directing career paths, assisting in reviewing and developing comprehensive training programs. **Process Improvement and Integration** * Engineering and Design Process Optimisation: Continuously improve and integrate MV system related engineering and design processes with the company's established "Kirby Way" for maximum efficiency and effectiveness. * Knowledge Sharing: Facilitate the sharing of best practices and lessons learned through workshops and training programs across regions. **Performance Management and Reporting** * Performance Monitoring: Monitor and track the progress and development of graduate electrical engineers. * Reporting and Auditing: Monitor and audit MV system compliance with established design, project engineering, and commissioning procedures through Project Support Reviews. * Project, Design and Engineering Reports: Support and assist in the development, maintenance, and accuracy of Project Engineering Reports for MV systems across multiple projects. **Collaboration and Communication** * Responsibilities Management: Work collaboratively with Project Leads, Project Managers, Design and Engineering Managers to clearly define the roles and responsibilities as a MV system owner. Depending on the scope and complexity of the system it could vary from full ownership to support in specific areas. * Training and Development Collaboration: Collaborate with HR and others to develop training and workshop materials (internal and external) on the MV system for engineer development programs. Develop or organise at least one training session per year. * Estimating Support: Assist the estimating team with technical queries and the evaluation of alternative technologies and value engineering proposals. * Interface with Procurement: Work with the procurement team to evaluate the suitability of products for alternative proposals. **Additional Responsibilities** * Engineering Alerts: Ensure the production of regular Engineering Alerts related to the MV system based on project findings for circulation across the entire group. Aim to produce at least one technical alert per year.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Marketing Associate
description: Roughly a third of all the food the world produces is thrown away, and after households, the kitchens that feed us in hotels, offices, hospitals and universities are among the biggest wasters of all. A single commercial kitchen can bin tens of thousands of euro of food a year without ever seeing it on a balance sheet. It is one of the largest, most solvable problems in climate today, and almost nobody can measure it. Positive Carbon makes it measurable. Founded in Dublin in 2020, we build AI that sees what a kitchen throws away. Our sensor sits above any bin and uses computer vision, the same technology found in self\-driving cars, to automatically capture the weight, volume and type of every item discarded, with no manual logging by busy kitchen staff. That turns an invisible operational habit into clear, real\-time financial data: what is being wasted, what it costs, and where to stop it. The results are real. Kitchens using Positive Carbon have cut the value of the food they waste by up to 90%, lifting gross profit by 2–8% and returning several times what they spend with us. We work with hospitality groups, universities and workplace caterers across Ireland, the UK and the DACH region And we are still small. That is the opportunity. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. We need a marketer who makes the sale faster, not a brand caretaker. Your first job is to build the assets that help our sales team and partners prove food\-cost leakage, build business cases and turn pilots into rollouts. This is a hands\-on product/partner marketing role — sustainability is a supporting message here, not the headline. The people we hire now will shape the product, the culture and the company with real ownership, a direct line to the founder, and a mission where, unusually, the economics and the planet pull in exactly the same direction. **Tasks** --------- * Produce case studies that lead with food\-cost outcomes context, contract type, baseline, actions, reduction, financial value, expansion. * Create partner enablement: co\-branded campaign kits, client one\-pagers, QBR materials, discovery aids. * Run focused segment campaigns (workplace, universities, hospitality) and measure campaign\-to\-meeting conversion. **Requirements** ---------------- * Self\-starter (must\-have): you've taken something from nothing to real, a project, a club, a side hustle, a campaign, and you like the kind of role where the path isn't mapped out for you. * Strong writing and the ability to build sales assets that actually get used. * Comfort with ROI and financial messaging. * Case\-study experience and an instinct for proof over polish. **Nice to have** * B2B SaaS or hardware\-plus\-software marketing experience. * Comfort being the first and only marketer in a company. **Benefits** ------------ * **Health Insurance** * **Pension** * **Bike to work** * **25 Days Annual Leave** * **Hybrid Work**

title: Mechanical & Electrical (Graduate)
description: Cairn Homes is looking for a graduate to join our Mechanical \& Electrical team, on\-site in one of our many locations. This will be an 18\-month FTC. **Key Responsibilities:** * Continuous monitoring of the installation quality for M\&E systems on residential projects, from initial coordination to final commissioning and handover * Support the site construction management teams in leading the coordination of M\&E systems and assist with onsite problem solving, if and when issues arise * Coordinate with project design teams as required for M\&E system interfaces with architectural and structural elements * Working with M\&E sub\-contractors onsite, ensuring programme and quality expectations are achieved * Liaise with Public Utility providers, local authorities, and design teams to ensure timely delivery of electricity, public lighting, gas, water and telecommunications services to new developments, acting as a point of contact for the respective sites * Anticipate the requirement for modification of existing site services and expedite mains service diversions in conjunction with the Utility Providers * Supplement the company’s innovation and value engineering processes in consideration and implementation of best\-in\-class mechanical / electrical systems and products * Management of the Temporary Electric / water systems **The Candidate;** * Further education in Mechanical, Electrical or Building Services * Interest in energy management and emerging trends in residential building services, design, and construction * Team player, organised, self\-motivated and ability to work on own initiative * Strong relationship builder, communication and collaboration skills required * Focus on deadlines and commitment to programme delivery * Willingness to travel between sites across greater Dublin and a full driving licence * Strong problem solving and attention to detail orientation * An ability to achieve demanding time and quality targets * The ability to work independently using their own initiative in an organised manner but also be a strong team player **Working with Cairn Homes** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits in Cairn which include * Competitive salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Full health insurance with Irish Life health * Annual salary review * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Free Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * Employee Assistance Programme with VHI * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture Committee * Talent Development Programmes * Approved Profit Sharing Scheme (APSS) The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it is not what we build, it is why we build. It is about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.

title: Mechanical Estimator
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe's only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. We are pleased to announce that we are currently seeking a **Mechanical Estimator** due to our continued success within the Data Centre Industry. The position will be based in our Head Office in Ballymount, Dublin. **Position:** Mechanical Estimator **Location:** Ballymount, Dublin 22 **Reporting to:** Lead Mechanical Estimator **Responsibilities** * Produce reports for management for bids and monthly meetings * Prepare estimates based on drawings and specifications * Manage quantities and prepare pricing document * Oversea pricing for elements of the building from the Irish and European subcontractors and suppliers * Utilise spreadsheets to compare subcontractors bids and quotations, and to demonstrate reasoning for selecting subcontractors * Assist in the maintenance of the cost data base * Present the estimate to the Estimating Director, and to the Board of Directors, where and when required * Build relationships with key vendors (subcontractors, suppliers, engineers etc) * Keep informed with developments in the construction industry * Participate in the development of procedures to ensure a high standard of quality control in the documents produced by the Estimating Department * Co\-operate with the application of quality assurance procedures * Mentor and train junior members of the team * Co\-ordinate and collaborate with the CSA \& Electrical Estimators **Requirements** * 4\+ years experience within a Mechanical capacity * Estimating or Quantity Surveying background * Knowledge of FIDIC contracts is desirable * Proficiency in the use of Microsoft Word, Excel, and in at least one of the more widely used QS BQ production software packages \- e.g. Buildsoft or CostX * Understand the requirement to achieve a high level of quality in the production of subcontractor tender documentation and Winthrop's tender submission * Be able to identify potential risks on a project * Be aware of the company's high standards of safety **Benefits** * Opportunity to work with a Market Leader in the Data centre Industry * Supportive career development * Competitive salary package * Collaborative team environment * Pipeline of project growth ***Winthrop Technologies Ltd is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Mechanical Supervisor (Finland)
description: **Mechanical Supervisor – Finland** We are currently recruiting a Mechanical Supervisor to work for a leading Engineering Contractor on a Hyperscale Data Centre project in Finland. If you are a Mechanical Supervisor with Data Centre experience who is looking for your next career move, please send your CV to Oisin O’Donohoe or call 086 085 7455 for a confidential discussion. **Responsibilities:** * Lead weekly toolbox talks to promote a strong safety culture and effective site communication. * Coordinate and supervise daily mechanical activities on site, ensuring works are completed safely, efficiently, and to programme. * Prepare weekly progress reports, highlighting achievements, challenges, and key project milestones. * Develop and manage two\-week lookahead schedules to ensure upcoming works are planned and resourced effectively. * Coordinate mechanical works with other trades and project stakeholders to maintain seamless site operations. * Ensure labour resources are aligned with project schedules and productivity targets. * Monitor and maintain exceptional quality standards across all mechanical installations and associated works. * Drive subcontractor performance, ensuring works are delivered in accordance with project requirements and company standards. * Communicate any variations, delays, or site issues promptly to the Project Manager. * Oversee document control, including drawings, RFIs, QA documentation, inspection records, and commissioning documentation. * Support commissioning activities, subcontractor management, and project handover processes. **Requirements** * Mechanical trade qualification or relevant Mechanical Engineering / Construction qualification. * 3\+ years’ experience in a Mechanical Supervisor, Foreman, or similar site leadership role with a main contractor. * Experience working on Data Centre, Pharmaceutical, Industrial, or other high\-tech construction projects preferred. * Strong leadership, organisational, and communication skills. * Ability to manage multiple subcontractors and coordinate site activities effectively. * Proficient in reporting, planning, and using cloud\-based project management systems. **Benefits:** * Salary €75,000 to €85,000 DOE. * Generous living away allowance paid in addition to salary. * Lucrative expat tax scheme. * Single occupancy accommodation and utility bills provided. * Regular return flights. * In\-country travel provided. * 24 days annual leave (rising to 30 days after 1 year's service) plus bank holidays. * Opportunity to work on Europe’s largest and most advanced Data Centre projects. * Excellent career progression and professional development opportunities within a fast\-growing sector. If you are a Mechanical Supervisor with Data Centre experience who is looking for your next career move, please send your CV to Oisin O’Donohoe or call 086 085 7455 for a confidential discussion.

title: Mid-Market Account Executive (Italian Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We’re looking for a Mid\-Market Account Executive who’s a people\-focused expert at identifying prospective mid\-market customers. With detailed knowledge of our full product suite, you'll match customers to the best plan based on their needs, helping them to adopt new features and increase usage of existing ones. title: Network Infrastructure Construction Quality Engineer
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain. You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers. In this role, you will: Work with construction teams to integrate quality assurance into data center network pathway infrastructure construction throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in construction design reviews, page turns, and reviews for construction documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during construction. You'll influence construction managers, general contractors, Colocation providers and relevant contractors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical construction milestone checks for pathway infrastructure. You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from construction teams and internal stakeholders to refine quality processes. You'll verify that pathway infrastructure construction meets installation requirements by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections. You'll document and align on construction standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and construction methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with construction managers, and provide guidelines to general contractors, Colocation providers and contractors to ensure consistent execution across all construction activities. This role matters because construction quality directly impacts data center reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in construction design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on. This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers. Key job responsibilities \- Own Quality Assurance related programs to drive world\-class quality in network infrastructure in the new construction of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Infra Quality Engineer \- Construction will be obsessed with quality, engineering, and design continuous improvements * Review cable tray designs against Amazon’s Policies and Industry Standards and define acceptance checklists for each build milestone * Partner with Construction Management team and Technical Infrastructure Project Manager to ensure network pathway infrastructures are built according to design documents and build project milestones, document early signals for risks and escalate to respective owners for remediations * Capture requirement variants between approved design and physical installation and collaborate with onsite teams and design teams to close gaps in pathway infrastructure installation methods. * Define and maintain updates of Quality Assurance documents including inspection checklists and installation requirements from design specifications, Quality Assurance standard operating procedures, Global Design Standards documents, Global Installation Best Practice Guidelines, Product Specifications for Inside Plant cable routing, and Quality Issue trackers. * Monitor installations and collect lessons learned on network pathway quality * Define Network Pathway Infrastructure Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support Construction teams and as reference guidelines for vendors. * Define quality and operational metrics related to construction performance, establish measurement frameworks, and analyze data to drive strategic improvements and inform decision\-making * Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to perform site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel A day in the life Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast\-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites. As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field * Experience carrying new design concepts through exploration, development, and into deployment or mass production * Experience with power management and power monitoring systems * Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in Electrical, Mechanical, Structural Engineering or equivalent field * Experience in root cause analysis and error correction, identifying changes to procedures and systems to implement long\-term fixes and avoid repeating issues * 4\+ years of experience in network pathway infrastructure design or installation in data center environments * Experience conducting quality inspections in live data center environments with engagement of contractors, engineers, and stakeholders **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Knowledge of engineering documentation, electrical diagrams and standard operating procedures * Knowledge of building codes and regulations including Life Safety, BOCA, NFPA, NEC, or OSHA * Experience in Data Center Engineering Operations, with a deep understanding of electrical and mechanical data center infrastructure * Experience reading and interpreting construction specifications and drawings for all domains * Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities, or experience in managing and troublshooting network Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

description: **Groupe Boutrais**, a major player in oyster and shellfish production and trading for over 20 years, is recruiting a **Broker / Commercial Specialist** for its trading subsidiary **Réseau Mer**, based in **Saint\-Coulomb (35\), Brittany**. title: Operations Engineer, AWS Managed Services
description: **Description** Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with recent examples of being named \#1 in customer service, \#1 most trusted, and \#2 most innovative. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in Cloud technologies. As a member of the AWS Support team you will be at the forefront of this transformational technology assisting a global list of companies that are taking advantage of a growing set of services and features to run their mission\-critical applications. You will work with leading companies in this space and directly with the engineering teams within Amazon developing these new capabilities. **Overview** The AWS Support team is seeking engineers that enjoy solving problems, working with customers, and have a strong technical background in application management and software development including Chef, Git, SVN, and at least one popular programming language. Do you have experience as a Systems Administrator working with Linux, Unix or Windows, and understand the advantages of working in a highly distributed virtual environment? Can you read and troubleshoot code snippets? If you fit the description, you might be the person we are looking for! **About** You will be surrounded by people who are smart, passionate about cloud computing, and believe that world class support is critical to customer success. Every day will bring new and exciting challenges on the job while you: * Learn and use ground\-breaking technologies * Troubleshoot application deployments, recreate customer issues, build proof of concept applications. Apply advanced troubleshooting techniques to provide unique solutions to our customers' individual needs * Build and maintain CloudFormation templates, Chef cookbooks and shell scripts to automate and deploy AWS resources and configuration changes * Collaborate and help build utilities and tools for internal use that enable you and your fellow AWS Engineers to operate safely at high speed and wide scale. * Leverage your day\-to\-day experiences to provide the voice of the customer to internal AWS teams * Drive customer communication during critical events * Write tutorials, how\-to videos, and other technical articles for the AWS customer community * Work on critical, highly complex customer problems that will span multiple AWS services **About The Team** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn't followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don't let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that's why customers from the most ambitious AI startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Your peers will work from different countries in EMEA but also globally. Here at AWS, it's in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there's nothing we can't achieve in the cloud. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience in technical support * Experience with virtualization, orchestration and cloud computing (eg. Hypervisors, VMware, Xen) * Experience managing full application stacks from the OS up through custom applications * Experience with strong project management skills and the ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with a scripting language such as PowerShell, Python, Bash, Perl, Ruby or similar * Knowledge of networking protocols such as HTTP, DNS and TCP/IP * Experience in a DevOps team, or experience setting up, maintaining and coordinating the automated patch and configuration management of production servers. * Experience with Chef, particularly writing Chef cookbooks to automate deployment and maintenance on Linux or Windows application servers Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
