title: Pizza Chef (Full-time)
description: Join the Team at Plúr Woodfired Pizza – Senior Pizza Chef Plúr Woodfired Pizza is looking for an experienced and passionate Senior Pizza Chef to join our growing team across our Naas and Clane locations in Co. Kildare. If you're passionate about great food, quality ingredients, and authentic woodfired pizza, we'd love to hear from you. Salary €34,000 – €38,000 per year Salary will be based on experience, skills, and responsibilities, and will be discussed during the interview and trial process. About Plúr At Plúr, we specialise in authentic Neapolitan\-style pizza, using local, high\-quality ingredients, naturally fermented dough, and traditional woodfired ovens. We are committed to consistency, craftsmanship, and creating a great experience for every customer. As we continue to grow, we're looking for someone who shares our passion for food quality, teamwork, and continuous improvement. The Role Working closely with our Executive Head Chef, you will play a key role in the day\-to\-day operation of our kitchens, ensuring exceptional food standards, smooth service, and strong kitchen leadership. This position involves working across both our Naas and Clane locations. Key Responsibilities * Leading kitchen operations during service * Preparing and cooking high\-quality pizzas to Plúr standards * Managing food ordering and stock control * Maintaining food cost and waste targets * Assisting with menu development and seasonal specials * Training and supporting junior kitchen team members * Ensuring HACCP and food safety standards are consistently met * Maintaining a clean, organised, and efficient kitchen environment * Working closely with management to improve systems and service standards What We're Looking For * Previous experience as a Pizza Chef, Senior Pizza Chef, or Sous Chef * Strong experience with dough preparation, fermentation, and woodfired ovens * Confidence leading a kitchen during busy service periods * Excellent organisational and communication skills * Strong attention to detail and consistency * HACCP certification * Positive attitude and ability to work as part of a team * A genuine passion for great food and hospitality Why Work With Us? * No late\-night finishes – service typically wraps up around 9:15pm * Competitive salary package * No 12\-hour shifts * Supportive and friendly working environment * Opportunities for growth and development within the business * Staff meals and team perks To apply, send your CV and a brief introduction outlining your experience and why you'd like to join the Plúr team. Follow us on Instagram: @plur\_woodfired\_pizza Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €34,000\.00\-€38,000\.00 per year Experience: * Pizza chef: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Planning Graduate - 2026 Suir Engineering Graduate
description: We have an exciting opportunity for Planning Graduate to join our 18\-month graduate programme. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark \& Sweden. **We Have Opportunities To Join Our Programme In** * Dublin Office * Waterford Office Travel will be required various sites as requested. **Our Graduate Programme** In addition to on the job experience we also provide some specialized programmes for our Graduate Trainees * Soft Skills Modules – communications / presentation skills * Career Pathway Conversations – learn how different parts of our business work * Group Projects \& Presentations * Site Visits **Is this Graduate Program for me?** * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree in relevant field * Analytical and problem\-solving skills with attention to detail. * A proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take on challenges. * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Flexibility to work on different sites and relocate if required. **What will I be doing?** * Continuously model the steps of the construction project in different diagrams. Monitor and report on project progress. * Make decisions related to logistics and resources. * Monitor \& Report on Internal productivity and issue Earned Value Analysis on a weekly basis. * Attend meetings with the Project Team to discuss progress made and any issues that arise over the course of the project. * Any other reasonable and relevant duties as requested by your manager, necessary to meet the ongoing needs of the company. * Daily \& weekly programme updates with the client. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * Salary starting from €40,000 per year * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions \- no waiting periods * Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave * Private Health Insurance contribution * Life and Disability cover * EAP for employees and their families * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer; we value our greatest asset …. our People. We do not require the support of Recruitment agencies at this time.

title: Process Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** **WE WANT YOU TO HELP US CREATE THE DIGITAL FUTURE OF OUR COMPANY** We are looking for exceptional people to join our diverse and multi\-cultural team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a lead role as we connect process, data and technology, creating sustainable business benefits and drive the achievement of Divisional, Sales Area and Common Function business outcomes. As we are creating a new operating model, this presents a great opportunity for learning and career development in this full\-time position. **We are now recruiting for a Process Specialist (Hybrid) \- for a permanent role in our Dublin Office.** **JOB PURPOSE** The purpose of the Process Specialist role is to represent the business interests and requirements in the Process area by bringing knowledge of current business operations and taking responsibility for helping define how the business will operate in the future, enabled by the new technologies implemented by the programme. This role manages changes that are required in the ‘Plan the Business’ functional area through solution of design, documentation of change, testing and coordinating of change through landscape and through to the deployment and end user training. In this role you will lead and support deployments and help to design, build, test and deploy the Plan the Business solution for Sandvik Mining BA, by working closely with cross stream team members, business members and applicable consulting partners and connected systems. The role requires a deep expertise in SAP and other connected planning connected systems. The role must demonstrate the ability to interpret business challenges across multiple programs and recommend the best solution and practices to improve the end\-to\-end transport solution and business processes. **MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES** * Perform the role of subject matter expert in Demand , Inventory and Supply Management within the ‘ Plan the Business ‘ process area and provide design and roadmap input for the process area. * Provide insight into how the business operates today and ensure that the requirements of the divisions / common functions are known and considered in the design process. * Coordinate system assessment discussions and related activities. * Contribute to and provide input to design and roadmaps for the process area. * Lead the implementation of ‘Plan the Business’ planning solutions, which may involve configuring the software, integrating it with other systems, and ensuring it aligns with business processes. * Peer reviewing change requests relating to the ‘Plan the Business’ space prior to moving forward to Business Area Design Authority approval. * Coordination, management \& approval of transport releases through the test landscape. * Prepare functional specifications for RICEFWs (Reports, interfaces, conversions, extensions, forms and workflows) and peer review as appropriate. * Actively own, promote and improve the technical solution and associated processes for your area. * Validate training material developed by the training team. * Support the Change Management team in assessing business change impacts and engaging with the business teams to drive adoption. * Provide input into resource planning. * Analysing proposed changes to business practices and advising senior management of any compliance implications. * Track, analyse and trend the overall performance of the Transportation Management system. Reviewing issues and completing root cause analysis to reduce issues reoccurring. * Other ad\-hoc duties as requested by management. **Experience** * 7\+ years solution experience, in both SAP and other planning solutions. * Strong knowledge of supply chain management processes and best practices. * Expertise in the configuration and implementation of SAP and other planning tools. * Prior work with integrations and exposure and experience with ETL layer and integration. * S4/HANA knowledge. * Logility Voyager or planning Tool exposure (IBP etc ) advantageous. * ABAP development and debugging knowledge is a plus. * Experience of agile delivery methodology for implementation * Strong communication skills with the ability to effectively interact at all levels within the company. **Education, Licenses \& Certificates** * Educated to degree level or equivalent. * Prior process leadership experience is preferred for role development. * Prior experience in agile methodology is advantageous. * Change management leadership experience is advantageous. * Proficient in MS Office Suite. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location**: Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to**: Business Process Manager \- Inventory Management **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply**? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is 17th June 2026\.** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU**.

title: Product Designer, Global
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Product Design team is made up of several groups that define, create, and deliver all user\-facing aspects of Stripe’s products. Product Designers work directly with Engineers, Product Managers, Data Scientists, and Marketers, as strategic partners, in each product area. In close collaboration with product teams, they shape user experiences, then translate them into high\-quality designs that can be tested, shipped, and refined. Designers are responsible for building elegant, functional products that users love—and want to tell others about. The Global design team crafts product experiences that make cross\-border selling as simple, performant, and scalable as domestic sales. Experiences empower businesses to easily expand into new markets worldwide without the friction of complex global operations, ranging from localized checkout experiences and comprehensive payment method coverage to handling global tax compliance and cross\-border money management. Because of its global reach, this team’s efforts drive significant impact for our users and business and navigate the unique challenges of designing for regions with dozens of currencies, languages, and regulatory frameworks. What you’ll do As a product designer, you’ll work closely with product managers and engineering teams to envision features in the Stripe Dashboard and checkout surfaces and partner with go\-to\-market teams to bring them to users. That includes new feature development, localization for priority geographies across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, and setting vision for our products to meet new user expectations in an increasingly globally connected and AI driven world. **Responsibilities** * Understand user workflows and pain points, and produce, test and iterate on high quality designs to solve user needs * Partner closely with product, engineering, marketing, and strategy partners to define a north star and product experience for new features * Prototype user experiences for user research testing and internal communication * Collaborate with teams across Stripe to craft a seamless dashboard user experience * Conduct UXR to test design decisions before shipping * Collaborate with our design systems teams to leverage and improve templates and components Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Has 6\+ years relevant design experience at a product\-driven tech company * Has strong craft demonstrated through a portfolio of work * Demonstrated skills in prototyping at a high fidelity using the latest AI tools * Has experience collaborating closely with engineering teams * Excellent written and verbal communication skills through design deliverables, videos, and written artifacts **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in a growth team driving user activation * Are excited about building collaborative relationships with many partners * Has a passion for turning complexity into usable, beautiful experiences * Are a strong design leader comfortable with bringing your point of view to a team Hybrid work at Stripe This role is available either in an office or a remote location (35\+ miles or 56\+ km from a Stripe office). In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This hits a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility about how to do this in a way that makes sense for individuals and their teams. Working remotely at Stripe A remote location is defined as being 35 miles (56 kilometers) or more from one of our offices. While you would be welcome to come into the office for team/business meetings, on\-sites, meet\-ups, and events, our expectation is you would regularly work from home rather than a Stripe office. Stripe does not cover the cost of relocating to a remote location. We encourage you to apply for roles that match the location where you currently live or plan to live. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €93,000 \- €139,600\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends. Hybrid work at Stripe This role is available either in an office or a remote location (35\+ miles or 56\+ km from a Stripe office). In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This hits a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility about how to do this in a way that makes sense for individuals and their teams. Working remotely at Stripe A remote location is defined as being 35 miles (56 kilometers) or more from one of our offices. While you would be welcome to come into the office for team/business meetings, on\-sites, meet\-ups, and events, our expectation is you would regularly work from home rather than a Stripe office. Stripe does not cover the cost of relocating to a remote location. We encourage you to apply for roles that match the location where you currently live or plan to live.

title: Product Development Associate
description: **JOB TITLE:** Product Development Associate: DIRECT TO RETAIL **LOCATION:** Dublin **REPORTS TO:** Manager Prod. Dev. EMEA – Home \& Fashion **SUMMARY OF POSITION :** Provides creative support for key DTR Fashion retailers through design briefs and point of view boards to increase 365 Fashion Business. Reviews designs and products submitted by key DTR Fashion retailer through WB product development/approval process across Fashion \& Home. Ensures submissions and product designs featuring any Warner Bros. owned properties are reviewed within a contractually stipulated timeframe to ensure compliance with Consumer Products policies. Works with Softline’s Hub to support \& produce compelling product which best represents WB brands at retail. Undertake research projects with the PD team to maintain knowledge of the competitive landscape. Support Creative in London with the creation of Mood boards, toolkits, comp shops, Fashion graphics to inspire key licensees as required Visit retailer offices as agreed to give onsite advice and approval. **JOB RESPONSIBILITIES :** * Work closely with DTR Softline’s Hub to achieve best results. * Provide creative support tailored to WBD business needs through DTR briefs and point of view boards to inspire key fashion retailers. * Provide concept CADS, design and product development support for key DTR fashion retailers where required. * Reviews samples and designs to ensure all submissions meet the brand guidelines and legal requirements. * Acts as the brand ambassador on all product submissions making decisions based on whether the product is in line with brand strategy, creative strategy, and product quality standards. * Approves or rejects based on the creative standard and legal guidelines set. * With knowledge of the manufacturing process and a knowledge of Warner Bros. policies and procedures and brand guidelines reviews and provides detailed comments on concept, pre\-production, and final samples submitted by licensees on items including product, packaging, marketing, point of sale. * Maintains a one\-on\-one relationship with EMEA counterparts, as well Designers, PD, Commercial, Franchise Teams etc. to ensure understanding and a smooth working approval process. * Drives creative, trend\-driven product initiatives and newness that contribute directly to agreed KPIs, revenue targets and wider business goals, in close partnership with commercial counterparts. **JOB QUALIFICATION STANDARDS** **Work Experience** * Minimum 3 years\-experience in product development, design. * Fashion and Home experience a must. * Design Degree or qualification preferred. * Experience working with Highstreet fashion retailers preferred. **Knowledge/Skills** * PC Skills: Windows, Microsoft Word and Excel required. Working Knowledge of Adobe Photoshop. Illustrator and In\-Design * Must have the ability to absorb and respond quickly to given information. * Must have the ability to learn the various guidelines relating to WB Intellectual properties and distinguish different styles of art from others to accurately log and review submissions. * Must have the ability to learn about art production and manufacturing processes in general. * Must be detailed and have an artistic eye. * Must have the ability to learn about handling digital and ai images. * Must have good spelling, grammar, punctuation, and English usage and be able to accurately describe images in words and accurately record all verbiage in the database correctly. * Must be able to sift through information and identify priorities. * Must be able to interface professionally with key fashion retailers, buyers, departments, licensees, and all levels of management. Other Requirements * Excellent organizational abilities. * Must have the ability to acquire and work with knowledge of copyrights and trademarks for use in product approvals. * Must be able to work independently. * Must be able to communicate effectively and tactfully with all levels of personnel, inside and outside of the company. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment and prioritize work effectively.

title: Product Growth Hacker (Exchange)
description: Crypto.com is on a mission to accelerate the world’s transition to cryptocurrency. As we continue to scale our Exchange App, we’re hiring a Growth Marketing Hacker to lead on our go\-to\-market strategy, lifecycle initiatives, and product positioning that fuel global user growth and engagement as we expand. This role sits at the intersection of product, data, and creative execution: ideal for a strategic marketer who thrives in fast\-paced, high\-ownership environments. You’ll work in close collaboration with product managers and growth teams to align feature rollouts, growth campaigns, and user insights into compelling narratives that resonate across geographies and user segments. **Responsibilities:** 1\. Strategy \& Positioning * Partner up with product and strategy teams to own the growth strategy for the Exchange (Advanced) vertical. * Develop stories and campaigns that differentiate our platform in a competitive global market. * Partner with data, analytics and product to define user personas (e.g. retail traders, crypto newcomers, high\-frequency traders) and map tailored journeys for each. * Lead on creative/copy positioning and brief submission for growth teams to deliver impactful and localised campaigns. * Work closely with product managers to align on roadmap priorities and customer feedback loops. 2\. Launch Execution * Partner up with the product team to manage the go\-to\-market planning for feature launches. * Conceptualise integrated marketing campaigns across in\-app, email, web, social, and earned media for UA to execute. * Partner with marketing, growth and product teams to ensure campaigns are timely, scalable, and data\-informed. * Coordinate with local market teams and growth to adapt messaging for region\-specific rollouts: localisation across product messaging, local behaviour and competitive landscape. 3\. Customer Insights \& Market Intelligence * Analyze market trends and conduct competitive benchmarking to identify opportunities and threats. * Run user interviews, feedback loops, and surveys to understand pain points and improve UX/messaging. * Feed findings back into product and growth teams to inform prioritization and positioning. 4\. Growth \& Retention * Design lifecycle programs that drive user activation, trading volume, and long\-term retention. * Conceptualise promotions, streaks, and incentive programs that reward engagement and boost retention. * Work with data, analytics and product teams to identify drop\-off points and optimize onboarding and reactivation flows. 5\. Performance \& Reporting * Work with Growth and Product to define KPIs across funnel metrics, retention, and campaign impact. * Report using tools like GA4, Tableau, Mixpanel: work closely with analytics and product to iterate quickly. **Requirements:** * 7\+ years of experience in product marketing: ideally crypto or financial background * Proven success launching and scaling B2C products or platforms and growing adoption and revenue sustainably. * Excellent communication and copywriting skills: you can simplify complex ideas and tell stories that convert. * Highly collaborative, with experience working cross\-functionally in fast\-paced environments. Bias to action. * Analytical mindset with proficiency in marketing analytics and user research. * Experience in regulated industries or financial services is a plus. We may use artificial intelligence tools to analyze the content of your Resume/CV against the specific requirements for the position. The purpose is to support our recruitment team in reviewing applications more effectively. These tools assist our recruitment team in their evaluation of your application by providing recommendations, but they do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans who consider the insights generated by the tools along with other relevant information. title: Project Coordinator GPOS Controls (12-Months FTC)
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **Project Co\-ordinator GPOS Controls** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Support the successful delivery of Global Point of Sale (GPOS) Controls by coordinating complex project activities, driving planning excellence, and ensuring strong governance and delivery discipline. * Prepare governance reporting packs, track project status, risks, issues, and dependencies, capture meeting minutes, and monitor completion of actions. * Develop and maintain integrated project and programme plans, milestones, dependencies, and deliverables, ensuring accurate updates in Planisware and POAP formats. * Proactively identify, track, and escalate risks, issues, assumptions, and dependencies through a centrally managed RAID log aligned across workstreams. * Operate structured change control processes, maintaining change request logs and managing impacts to scope, timeline, and approvals. * Act as a trusted advisor to workstream leads, build strong stakeholder relationships, and drive continuous improvement through automation (e.g., Copilot) and lessons learned. **What You’ll Get** This role is a hybrid opportunity, offering 3 days Working from home. People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Pension, 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 2–5 years of experience supporting technology‑enabled transformation programmes, with a strong grasp of software delivery lifecycles, project management methodologies, and emerging technologies. * Hands‑on experience using project management and delivery tools such as Planisware, Jira, or Microsoft Project, alongside data analysis and reporting tools including Excel, Power BI, or Tableau. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate complex technical and financial information to both technical teams and senior stakeholders. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capabilities, enabling effective identification of issues and development of practical solutions in fast‑paced environments. * Proven planning, organisation, and multitasking skills, with the ability to prioritise workloads and deliver to agreed timescales. * Collaborative team player with the confidence to challenge constructively, complemented by desirable experience in Retail domains (e.g. Supply Chain, Digital, Store Operations) and project management certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, APM, Scrum Master, or SAFe. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-7817

title: Python Developer
description: Our client is a major multinational with an established presence in Dublin, Ireland and a major technology focused development centre looking to hire a **Python Developer** on a daily rate contract. Strong possibilities for renewal with major projects ongoing for many years. This is great opportunity to work on critical projects with a global impact as a key individual contributor supporting the team with your expertise as a contractor. If you would like to know more click apply **now!**

title: QC BioAnalytical Sciences
description: **QC BioAnalytical Sciences** This job specification outlines the general responsibilities associated with the role of QC BioAnalytical Sciences \- Senior Associate on the Client Manufacturing site. This role will be responsible for leading, coordinating, facilitating, and undertaking activities pertaining to lab operations and compliance, with minimal supervision, acting as a role model for other lab colleagues. The role undertakes implementing continuous improvement projects and supporting a lean culture and Right First Time (RFT). The role also represents the QC unit by liaising with internal and external customers. Additionally, the role may involve carrying out additional work functions that are not described in this specification but are associated with their role. **Key Responsibilities:** * Experience in a wide variety of analytical techniques including but not limited to cell based\- Bioassays, Immunoassays, Cell Culture (use of cells to execute bioassays) and Aseptic Technique. Experience with liquid\-handling robots is also preferable. * Plan and perform multiple, complex routine/ non\-routine methods and procedures and a large variety of assays. * Participate in the peer review of analytical data. * Responsible for providing technical guidance and applying expertise and critical thinking to help to resolve technical issues. * Lead training of staff on technical aspects of job as required. * Develop, revise and implement procedures that comply with appropriate regulatory requirements. * Qualification of analytical equipment and related testing functions. * Participate in Analytical Method Transfers * Compliance with Standard Operating Procedures and Registered specifications. * Ensure the laboratory is operated in a safe manner * Maintain quality systems within the QC laboratories to ensure ongoing compliance to cGLP and cGMP. * Ensure timely completion of Laboratory Investigation Reports and deviations through the Non\-Conformance procedures. * Participate in the generation and update of SOP's, trend data, investigations, non\-conformance's, validation protocols, reports, method validation/verification's and equipment qualifications. * Participate in regulatory agency inspections as required. * Plan and implement procedures and systems to maximise operating efficiency. * Manage and contribute to the achievements of department productivity and quality goals. Requirements * Bachelors degree in a science discipline * Microbiology degree acceptable, only if clear and direct experience with bioassays/immunoassays is present * Strong experience in Cell based potency bioassays min experience, Cell culture, GMP/GDP Elisa ( minimum 6 months, ideally over 1 year) * Hands\-on cell culture skills * Direct experience with immunoassay techniques, especially ELISA * Familiarity with GMP/GDP lab environments * Prior involvement in bioassays/immunoassays, not entry\-level (someone who can hit the ground running with minimal ramp\-up) * CVs should evidence routine lab testing, method execution, data analysis for the above * Experience in additional project\-based lab responsibilities (e.g., Lean/CI, error management, SOPs, method transfers) * Comfort with digital lab systems (e.g., Lens Smart Lab) * Experience with multi\-functional teams in a large QC setting

title: Quality Assurance Associate
description: This role supports the QA function with the Management of Food Safety systems for the site. The QA function is responsible for the development and administration of programs and policies that result in company\-wide compliance with regulatory agency requirements. The main responsibility of the QA Role initially is the expansion, implementation and maintenance of the Quality Management systems to new areas of site. Other duties include supporting the documentation internal auditing, CAPA systems for the business. Ability to work in cross\-functional teams is critical, within a high\-volume production environment **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES \& DUTIES** * Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of Food Safety Management Systems in the production areas through GMP audits, CCP verifications and Food Safety walks. * Conduct regular audits and inspections to ensure compliance with company policies, processes and FSSC 22000 standards. * Expand and implement current Quality Management Systems into new areas of site. * Work closely with other departments to ensure integrated quality management. * Collaborate and provide guidance and support to help operations team and other departments to understand and implement quality standards. * Assist with development and delivery of the Food Safety training program for existing and new employees. * Assist with internal and facility 3rd party audit requirements. * Act as administrator for the Document Management system, to manage the full suite of company documentation. * Training of colleagues on the requirements around documentation. * Keep up to date with the regulatory requirements around documentation and implementing changes as necessary. * Cross\-functional teamwork to meet project targets. * Drive, monitor and review corrective actions, supporting the corrective and preventative action processes. * Must have regular, punctual attendance. * Other duties as assigned. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance Application question(s): * Do you have a working knowledge of Quality Assurance systems and GMPs? Education: * Bachelor's (preferred) Experience: * food industry quality control: 2 years (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Quality Assurance Associate
description: **About Your New Employer** * Join a long\-established leader in the food and beverage industry. * Work at a high\-volume production facility committed to quality, safety, and continuous improvement. * Be part of a supportive team culture in a business that values collaboration, growth, and high standards. **About Your New Job** * As the Quality Assurance Associate, **you** will support the expansion, implementation, and maintenance of food safety and quality management systems across the site. * **You** will conduct GMP audits, CCP verifications, Food Safety walks, and regular inspections to ensure compliance with internal and FSSC 22000 standards. * **You** will work cross\-functionally to integrate quality management practices and deliver food safety training to both new and existing employees. * **You** will oversee the administration of the Document Management System, ensure documentation remains current with evolving regulations, and drive corrective and preventative action (CAPA) processes. * **You** may assist with internal and third\-party audits and support teams in implementing quality standards and process improvements. **What Skills You Need** * A degree (or equivalent) in Quality Assurance or a science discipline. * Minimum 2 years’ experience in food industry quality control or assurance. * Practical knowledge of Quality Assurance systems, GMPs, FSSC 22000, and strong attention to detail. * Proven experience creating procedure documents and training employees. * Proficiency with MS Office Suite (especially Excel); highly organised, proactive, and able to work independently or as part of a team. * Excellent time management, project delivery, and communication skills. **What's on Offer** * Opportunity to advance your expertise in food safety and quality management in a dynamic, growth\-oriented facility. * Full\-time, permanent role with ongoing training and career development support. * Be recognised for your contributions in a collaborative, high\-performing environment. * Competitive salary (details on application) and comprehensive benefits. **What's Next** Apply now by clicking the “Apply Now” button or contact Lir directly on 014744648 for more details. If this role isn’t quite right but you’re seeking something similar, get in touch—we have multiple opportunities in Quality Assurance and related areas.

title: Receptionist - Front Of House - Baggot Street, D2
description: **Who We Are** Truly Dental is a modern dental company operating across Ireland, the UK, and the UAE, dedicated to high\-tech, compassionate, quality dental care. On our Baggot Street, Dublin 2 clinic **,** we deliver cutting\-edge dentistry in a welcoming environment. **Our Impact \- Fast Facts** * Flagship network covering Ireland, the UK \& UAE. * 30\+ treatment rooms at a major Dublin site (indicative of the network's capacity and scale). * On\-site lab \& digital radiography/CBCT enabling timely diagnostics and treatment. * Extended opening hours (Mon\-Sun) to maximise patient access in city\-centre and regional sites. **Purpose of the Vacancy** As a Front of House Team Member at Baggot Street, Dublin 2, you will be the welcoming first point of contact for patients and play a key role in delivering five\-star service, supporting appointment flow, patient check\-in/out, phone and inbox management, and helping ensure that clinical teams can deliver outstanding care. **Key Responsibilities** * Warmly greet, welcome, and check in/out patients; provide clear, empathetic information at all times. * Manage a multiline phone system and highvolume inbox/queries; prioritise between in\-person and remote patient needs. * Schedule, reschedule, and confirm appointments; coordinate with clinical teams to optimise diaries. * Prepare patient charts and documentation for dental staff; update and maintain accurate patient records. * Generate billing statements, process payments, reconcile daily cash takings, and handle deposits / clerical handoff as required. * Operate clinic software (training provided; network uses EXACT practice management system) and adhere to data\-protection protocols. * Support changeover periods and occasional weekend shifts as required; collaborate with the team to maintain service across full opening hours. * Uphold Truly's hospitality standards and contribute actively to a friendly, cooperative team culture. **Requirements \- Competencies \& Skills** **Education** * Secondary education required; vocational/admin qualification is an advantage. **Experience** * Minimum 1\-2 years in front\-of\-house or reception roles; dental or hospital reception experience strongly preferred. * Proven success in high\-volume, customer\-facing environments. **Knowledge \& Skills** * Experience with multiline phone handling, appointment scheduling, billing, and cash reconciliation. * Proficient or willing to learn clinic practice management systems (Dentally experience is a plus). * Strong multitasking, organisation, and attention to detail in a busy setting. * Excellent spoken and written English; able to communicate clearly and empathetically with patients from diverse backgrounds. * Comfortable working closely with clinicians and senior specialists; professional discretion and awareness of GDPR/data protection requirements. **Core Competencies** * Patient\-centric service \& empathy * Stress tolerance \& prioritisation in a high\-volume environment * Teamwork \& cross\-functional coordination * Accountability \& reliability (punctuality, accuracy of cash/records) * Learning agility (adapting to systems \& processes) **What We Offer** * Attractive part\-time terms and salary for the right candidate; overtime paid as applicable. * Career progression opportunities: admin support, training, and guidance in a fast\-growing dental network. * A fun, modern working environment with up\-to\-date dental software and processes. * Training on Dentally system for the successful candidate, supporting your growth in the role. * Flexibility and variety: part\-time hours, weekend shifts, opportunity to support across clinic flow and service. **Important Details Before Applying** * Full\-time role based at Baggot Street, Dublin 2 \- full flexibility required, including weekends. * Shift pattern will involve rotation across early/late appointments to meet clinic hours; exact schedule to be agreed.

title: Resident Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary** We are seeking a motivated Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Glass Bottle, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Sandymount Strand, Co. Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, leasing support and property management activities. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents, prospective tenants, visitors and third\-party maintenance contractors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Reservations Agent, Customer Service Agent, Sales Advisor, Front of House, Front Office / Reception Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** Customer Service \& Resident Relations: * Policy and Standards Adherence: Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * Issue Resolution: Address resident complaints, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * Resident Engagement Initiatives: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * Resident Support: Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. Leasing Support: Assist the Leasing team with enquiries and viewings. * Enquiries: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties. * Viewings: Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property. * Administrative Support: Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities** * Property Support: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * ‘Stock’ Coordination: Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. * Inspections: Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * Administrative Support: Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties. **Targets And KPIs** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities** * Attention to Detail: Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * Proactive and Initiative\-Driven: Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). * Flexibility: Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude: Critical in managing teams and delivering top service to residents (essential). **Skills** * Communication Skills: Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * Problem\-Solving: Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * Customer Service: Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * Computer Literacy: While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired)** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Professional certifications (desired) such as: * The Property Institute (TPI) or IRPM Membership. Remuneration / Bonuses / Benefits * Base salary: €35,000 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes daytime shift work (between 07:00 – 23:00\) Location: This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location (primarily at Glass Bottle, Sandymount Strand, Dublin 4 but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process.

title: Retail Betting Assistant
description: **Starting Salary: €14\.40 per hour** **Your Role in the Team?** Are you a proactive and motivated individual who is passionate about achieving results? Do you have the ability to meet shop goals and targets? Don't worry if you've never worked in the betting industry before, we will provide full training… but it's your attitude that counts! Does this sound like you? If so, keep reading! As a Retail Betting Assistant at Paddy Power, you will be the welcoming host for our customers, ensuring they have an outstanding experience in our retail shops. Your role will be to engage warmly with every visitor, providing best\-in\-class service and creating a friendly atmosphere. Your enthusiasm for technology will help you promote our online and retail products and services, ensuring that each customer enjoys their time with us. Join our team and be a key player in delivering outstanding hospitality in the dynamic world of retail betting! **How you’ll do it?** We want people who will exceed customer's expectations, building effective relationships with customers, creating a warm and positive environment which they love to be in. This will primarily involve hosting our customers in shop, cross selling our retail and online products and services, clearly communicating answers to customer queries and informing customers about our latest promotions. Other duties will include: * Assisting customers to place bets through various channels, such as our betting terminals, telephone services, and in\-shop transactions * Maintaining excellent shop presentation standards to create an inviting atmosphere for customers * Stepping up and guiding operations in the absence of the Shop and Deputy Manager, ensuring smooth functioning of the shop * Adapting to different situations while remaining customer\-focused * Fulfilling social responsibility duties by adhering to established guidelines and regulations **What we offer?** * Competitive salary including increase after 12 months. * Additional premiums for overtime, Sundays, and bank holidays. * Paid Rest breaks. * Flexible Work Patterns * Performance Bonus \& Promotional rewards * Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity, Adoptive, Parental Leave \& Wedding Leave) * Company Sharesave Scheme (25% discount on Flutter Shares) * Life Cover * Company Pension Scheme. * Company Sick Pay * Wedding leave. * Discounted Health Insurance. * Employee Assistant Programme (including up to6 free counselling sessions) * Financial Wellbeing platform – NUDGE. * Company Social Events. * Group Discount Scheme * Eyecare vouchers * Cycle to work scheme * Tax Saver Tickets (Bus/Rail) **And the Values that guide us? Here they are:** **Customer First, Always \| Change The Game \| Win Together \| Free To Be Me** We’re proud of our 4 Values, they reflect what matters most to our people and will guide the decisions we make, represent what we believe in and support how we do things at Paddy Power. **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.** **If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.**

title: Risk Trader
description: **The Role** The Risk Trader will be responsible for the trading and risk management of all our horse \& greyhound racing content for all Flutter UK\&I facing brands. The successful candidate will also be tasked with providing an industry leading antepost, specials and extra market offering for our greyhound racing product. They will be responsible for the horse racing risk management tasks and the day to day running of the greyhound desk. **What you will be doing** : **Risk Management** * To effectively analyse bet information to drive pricing accuracy * To relay risk information in real time to the horse racing traders * You will also be required to conduct retrospective risk reports and post trading analysis **Customer Management** * To effectively manage our customer base and tailor the product offering to the needs of our customers * You will be required to manage customer level risk and liabilities including bet clearance **Greyhound Proposition** * To price all domestic UK\&I greyhound races daily and ensure we have a compelling proposition in the marketplace for all our brands * To offer industry leading specials and extra markets to drive customer engagement * To keep up to date on antepost competitions and industry developments **Effective Communication** * To work within the high paced trading environment and effectively react to and communicate real time risk information * You will be required work within the wider racing team liaising on risk management decisions and wider racing projects **Technical Skills** * Strong Microsoft office user particularly Excel and Powerpoint. * Excellent presentation skills. * Strong report\-writing skills, with a keen eye for detail. **What We're Looking For...** **Skills \& Experience Required:** * Customer focused – thinks about customers 24/7 * Effective collaborator – works with others within the team and the wider organisation, whilst communicating effectively across a range of channels and audiences * Ability to work at pace and on own initiative * Makes quality decisions – makes confident decisions under pressure, which fundamentally drive results and high levels of customer satisfaction. * Highest levels of integrity * Drives results **About PaddyPower** **Work that makes play jealous** Paddy Power title: SMB Account Executive (DACH) — London
description: **Job Description** Job Description **Join The Team Redefining How The World Experiences Design.** Hello, hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte! Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. **Where And How You Can Work** This role is based in **London at our European HQ in Hoxton Square, Shoreditch.** You'd be working on a hybrid schedule, in the office with your team regularly, with flexibility around that. We believe in trusting people to find the balance that works for them. With all kinds of teams based here, plus event spaces to gather our team and communities. You’ll experience a warm welcome from our Vibe team at front of house, amazing home cooked food from our Head Chef and a variety of workspaces to hang out with your team mates or get solo work done. If you're based in Ireland and considering the move to London, we can support your work visa application. Relocation costs would be yours to cover, but we'll help make the visa side as straightforward as possible. **What You’d Be Doing In This Role** Our Account Executives work closely with our customers to understand their goals and provide tailored solutions to meet their needs. Whether it's creating eye\-catching pitch decks for their sales teams, designing creative marketing materials at scale, or ensuring everyone is on\-brand, our team has the expertise to help our customers in all departments succeed. You'll own a targeted book of SMB prospects, run an end to end sales cycle from initial outreach to close, and contribute directly to revenue generation. Leveraging the SPICED methodology, you'll qualify opportunities effectively, uncover customer value, and drive scalable, repeatable sales motions. This is a high impact role, ideal for someone who thrives in a fast growing environment, enjoys autonomy, and is energized by building pipeline, closing deals, and shaping Canva’s presence in Europe. **At The Moment, This Role Is Focused On** * You’ll conduct outbound sales activities to identify, prospect, and engage with SMB clients. * You’ll develop a deep understanding of SMB customers' needs, pain points, and objectives to tailor Canva's solutions effectively. * You’ll proactively reach out to potential SMB clients via phone, email, and other channels to present Canva's value proposition and product offerings. * You’ll build and maintain strong, long\-term relationships with SMB clients through regular communication, follow\-up, and exceptional customer service. * You’ll collaborate closely with internal teams, including Sales \& Success, Marketing, and Product, to optimize the sales process and ensure a seamless customer experience. * You’ll meet and exceed sales targets and performance metrics, including monthly quotas and conversion rates. * You’ll keep accurate and up\-to\-date records of sales activities, customer interactions, and pipeline management using our CRM system. * You’ll stay informed about industry trends, competitive landscape, and product developments to effectively position Canva in the SMB market segment. **You're probably a match if** * You have a proven track record of success in outbound sales or account management roles, preferably in a B2B or SMB\-focused environment. * You have strong sales acumen with excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills. * You have a results\-oriented mindset with a passion for driving revenue growth and delivering exceptional customer experiences. * You have the ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and manage time effectively in a fast\-paced, target\-driven environment. * You have familiarity with CRM and outreach software (e.g., Salesforce, Outreach IO, Sales Navigator and Gong) * You're fluent in German. (this is a must) * You have prior experience in the tech industry, SaaS sales, or working with SMB clients is a plus. **About The Team** Our Sales and Success team support Canva Enterprise, with the mission to empower every organization to design. Canva Enterprise lets organizations consolidate design, content production, and collaboration tools under one secure and centralized account. From whiteboards to docs, to presentations and our AI platform magic Studio, our Sales team work with all departments empowering them to create and collaborate at scale. **What's in it for you?** Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard \- and we do \- but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work. **Here's a Taste Of What's On Offer** * Equity packages \- we want our success to be yours too * Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents \& carers * An annual Vibe \& Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup \& more * Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info. **Other Stuff To Know** We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process. We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above \- we still want to hear from you! Please note that interviews are conducted virtually

title: Sales Associate
description: **About the role** You will own the full sales cycle — from first conversation to signed contract. That means you prospect, qualify, pitch, negotiate, and close. You'll build and manage your own pipeline across new and existing accounts, partnering closely with our account, delivery, and practice teams to bring the right solution to every client conversation. This role involves a high level of internal partnership amongst many different teams you'll be the key link between marketing, sales, consulting, and product teams. **Day to day, you'll:** * Hunt for new business. Identify, target, and open net\-new accounts across enterprise and mid\-market — you don't wait for inbound * Run deals end to end. From discovery through to MSA and SOW, you own the commercial process and know how to move a deal forward at every stage * Build and leverage partner relationships. You'll work shoulder to shoulder with AWS, Salesforce, and Microsoft to generate joint pipeline and bring co\-sell opportunities to life * Win on value, not price. You'll define win themes, understand the competitive landscape, and contribute to compelling proposals that land * Hold yourself accountable to a number. You'll manage a well\-structured, growing book of business and have the forecasting discipline to back it up **What You'll Bring** * A proven track record in solution sales — you've hit your number, you know what it takes, and you're hungry to do it again * Hunter mentality. You open doors others don't and you follow through until they close * An AI\-first mindset, both for the sales life cycle and in how we will deliver our solutions * The ability to build real relationships — with clients, with partners, and with the internal team you'll rally around a deal. * Sharp commercial instincts — you understand how to structure a deal, handle an objection, and negotiate to a win * A collaborative edge. You know that in a team\-selling culture, the best closers bring people with them * A passion for sales and technology * Excellent collaboration and team\-building skills * Organized and methodical; strong prioritization skills * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to detail * Proactive self\-starter attitude and a willingness to learn. * Strong relationship building skills; internally and with clients. * Proficient with and excited to use next\-generation AI tooling across all domains of the go\-to\-market workflow * Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint Copilot) **Nice to have** * Sales industry experience in Financial Services, Healthcare/Life Sciences, Manufacturing, Technology * Partner relationship manage ﻿ ment within the tech eco system (AWS/MSFT/Salesforce) We have a question for you \- and it’s something we’re really passionate about. Can you imagine a world in which you can truly love your life and your work? Well, we have some good news – creating that world and making this vision a reality is what we get out of bed for; it’s our north star. But what do we really mean when we talk about loving your life and your work? Let’s take a look at the ways in which we help our team members to achieve this – and the ‘how’. **Deep connections, better outcome** We have deep relationships with over 400 leading technology partners and they love us for our innovative and outcome based approach. Our people are passionate about solving our clients’ problems using the tech that’s the best solution for them. What’s more, we’re there to work side\-by\-side with our client teams to enable them for success long after we’ve gone. We’re all about momentum that outlasts us. **Flexibility** Life is busy and we appreciate that. We’re often juggling work, family, and personal commitments. We do everything we can to support our people in prioritising what matters to them while also working on high\-impact projects that they’ll love. **People\-first** Great solutions start with great people. And those great people are at their best when they’re empowered to be their true authentic selves. Through leading with kindness and empathy, and striving for equity, we’re able to create better experiences for our people and our clients. Our culture is central to everything we do \- encouraging passion and adventure, adaptiveness, and diversity of thought. Inclusion, diversity and equity is at the top of our agenda, we have created a community where we empower our team to be the best version of themselves. **Rewards** There’s no shying away from it \- the compensation and benefits on offer have to be competitive too, right? We know that. That’s why we have a dedicated team working with our leaders to ensure our packages are fair, competitive, and rewarding! Take a look at the role below and if something sparks your interest, apply! Want to learn more? Get in touch! If you require any assistance with regards to reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please do not hesitate to contact us – we will always be happy to help.

title: Sales Engineer
description: Help us drive revenue growth! You win when our AE wins. We're looking for our first dedicated sales engineer to convert Tiptap users and prospects into customers. You will work closely with the entire team, sharing your unique insights from sales meetings and your daily work with users to help us continue building something people want. **The core problem we're solving** Millions of people and AI agents around the world are working with the same thing right now: text documents. Humans interact with them through a rich text editor in the browser. Agents interact with them through APIs and structured data like JSON. While the vast majority of users have no idea about the underlying technology, and even most web\-savvy people think of a text editor as a simple piece of HTML, most of the content on the web comes through them. And while it always seems like an easy problem to solve for web developers at first, it's extremely difficult and finicky to do well, even in the age of agentic coding and especially when you need to build more sophisticated agentic document workflows or editor UIs like Google Docs or Notion do. Let's describe it like this: building document editing tools yourself feels like a Rubik's Cube that's almost solved. But if you don't know the formula for solving a Rubik's Cube, every turn you make makes it worse while you think you're getting closer. At some point it breaks you. That's why we built Tiptap. Transforming this complexity into fun. **About Tiptap** Chances are high that you've already used Tiptap without knowing it in one of the apps you have open daily. With 37k GitHub stars and over 45M npm downloads per month, Tiptap is the most popular open\-source editor framework worldwide, used by tens of thousands of businesses such as Anthropic, Spotify, and GitLab to build advanced content editing experiences into their apps. With our paid offerings, we are striving to build the leading ecosystem of services around rich text documents, created and processed by humans and AI agents. Our mission is to shape the future of how content is created on the web. Sounds too bold? Indeed, it is! **In a nutshell** \- Role: Sales Engineer \- Employment Type: Full\-time, permanent \- Location: 100% remote in Dublin, London, or US East (US citizen/visa only) \- Salary: $90,000 – $120,000 annually \- Annual Vacation: 30 days \- Annual Training Budget: $1,000 \- Employee Stock Options: 0\.1 – 0\.3% \- Equipment: MacBook, monitor \& accessories \- Inflation Compensation: 2% annual salary increase to compensate for average inflation \- Office Retreat: Fully paid one\-week company offsite each year **Your impact** Your daily work affects how businesses around the world succeed with Tiptap. This huge responsibility comes with one major benefit: impact! Every call you join, every demo you build, every prospect you unblock turns into revenue, into products our customers ship, and into the signal that shapes our roadmap. **Our Core Values** Open Source: We believe in the power of open source and community collaboration. Transparency: We share knowledge openly. Autonomy and Impact: Your work directly influences the experience of every person who develops with Tiptap (which is a lot). Problem\-Solving: We encourage rapid iteration and continuous learning to solve the web's biggest challenges. High agency: We're active, resourceful, and biased toward action to find or create ways to achieve goals. **Our Tech Stack** To make sure you don't regret joining Tiptap, here's our tech stack: \- Languages: Java \- Frameworks: Zend, jQuery, AngularJS \- Databases: Oracle Hell, just kidding! You'll find the real expectations below in Qualifications. **Hackathons** Four times a year, we host five\-day hackathons in person at our Berlin HQ, travel covered. The purpose of the hackathon is to 1\) strengthen our team culture, 2\) hack and experiment with cutting\-edge technologies and use cases around content editing, and 3\) celebrate our achievements. **Responsibilities** \- Represent the technical side on customer calls alongside our AE, Tim, mostly with US prospects \- Build live demos and code samples that move active deals forward \- Run technical deep\-dives with prospect engineering teams \- Guide prospects who don't yet know what to build. Most don't, and that's where you create value \- Feed customer signal back into product, closing the loop on the roadmap **Qualifications** \- You're proficient in JavaScript / TypeScript and can write clean code and prototype customer\-facing applications quickly. \- You've got hands\-on React and Node.js experience across frontend and backend. Enough to build a convincing live demo, debug a prospect's integration on a call, and answer technical questions with depth. \- You have proven customer\-facing experience in a technical role like Sales / Solutions Engineering, DevRel, Forward Deployed Engineering, or as a Founding Engineer or technical co\-founder. \- AI\-native daily workflow: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or similar tools are an established part of how you work. \- You've integrated LLM APIs (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, or similar) hands\-on, ideally in a customer\-facing or product context. \- Native or near\-native English. The vast majority of customer conversations are conducted in English. \- Excellent written and verbal communication. Clear, precise, and able to translate complex technical topics for an engineering audience. \- A strong sense of ownership: you take initiative, structure your own work, and bring clarity to ambiguity in an early\-stage environment. *Bonus* \- You have used Tiptap, ProseMirror, Yjs, Lexical, Slate, or other rich\-text frameworks in production. \- You've owned a customer relationship end to end, not just the code: your own company (exit, pivot, or honest flameout), a paid product you sold and supported, or an OSS project people depend on. \- You have written publicly: blog, conference talks, Reddit. \- You're familiar with collaborative editing, CRDTs, or real\-time sync architectures. **Here's how it works** Sounds like a good fit? Write why you want to work with us. We would be more than proud if you would like to be part of our team, and we are looking forward to meeting you soon! IMPORTANT: Tell us in your application if you prefer tabs or spaces. We won't judge you, we promise! ***Next steps:*** 1\. Apply: We will review your application and typically respond to your email within 5 days. 2\. Intro Call: If we like your application, we will have a 30\-minute video call to explore mutual fit. 3\. Deep Dive: If we both want to take it to the next level, we will have a 45–60\-minute video call to talk about sales and engineering in detail and your past achievements. 4\. Trial Day: You'll join our team for a day (paid) to work on a task to provide you with a realistic view of what it's like to work at Tiptap, and of course we're more than curious to get your honest feedback! 5\. Offer: We aim to make a decision and offer no later than a few days after your trial day to get you onboarded quickly. **Your first time at Tiptap** We'll get you set up before day one, equipment and welcome package included, so you're ready to go from the start. Day one you're already in real sales calls next to our AE and engineers, learning the product and the customers fast. Your first month is hands\-on: building demos, writing code, talking to prospects, owning it from the start. We look forward to receiving your application!
